title: Adobo Black Bean Tostadas With Shredded Lettuce, Lime Cashew Crema, Avocado & Salsa Macha
method:
gut health Adobo Black Bean Tostadas With Shredded Lettuce, Lime Cashew Crema, Avocado & Salsa Macha Our smokey, slow-cooked black beans are layered with a multitude of complex flavours like cumin, paprika and ancho chillies, served on crispy corn tostadas and topped with crisp shredded lettuce and creamy avocado. Finished with a zingy lime cashew crema and our house made salsa macha featuring crushed peanuts and three types of Mexican chillies. Prep time 2 Total time 10 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Take out a saucepan and preheat your oven to 200°C / 180°C fan. STEP 2 Traditionally tostadas are shallow fried in oil for 30 seconds on each side, which you can do if you want them to turn really crispy. However for our healthier, oil-free version, simply pop the tacos onto a baking tray with space in between them and bake for 8 minutes or until crispy, flipping half way. STEP 3 Drain the black beans and empty into your saucepan along with the smoky adobo sauce , place over a medium heat and warm through, stirring occasionally, until piping hot, around 5 minutes. Add a splash of water if it starts to stick at the bottom. Roughly mash around half of the beans with a fork and set aside. STEP 4 Finely shred the baby gem by cutting horizontally across the head. De-stone the avocado and cut into thin slices flat side down. If you're feeling fancy you could fan the avocado for a more elevated final presentation. Using the palm of your hand, carefully push the avocado down and away from yourself to open it up like a fan without the slices breaking. Cut the lime into wedges. STEP 5 Lay everything out on your table and then start building your tostadas. Start with a layer of black beans , then shredded lettuce , dress the lettuce with a drizzle of cashew crema , top with some slices of avocado and a final drizzle of salsa macha . Finish with a squeeze of fresh lime juice, and enjoy! What’s in your box Smoky Adobo Sauce Black Beans Salsa Macha Freshly Baked Tacos Cashew Crema Baby Gem Lettuce Avocado Lime Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. Calories 753kcal Total fat 48.4g Saturated fat 8.3g Salt 1.5g Total carbohydrate 64.9g Total sugar 13g Fibre 19.4g Protein 21.3g Ingredients / Allergens Black beans, red onion, garlic, cumin, smoked sweet paprika, ground coriander, cinnamon, clove, oregano, tomato purée, raw organic agave nectar, chipotle paste, balsamic vinegar ( sulphites ), water, sea salt, cashews ( nuts ), organic cultured soy yoghurt ( soy ), fresh lemon juice, garlic powder, extra virgin olive oil, rapeseed oil, ancho chiles, guajillo chiles, roasted peanuts , sesame seeds, apple cider vinegar ( sulphites ), corn tortillas (yellow corn flour), baby gem lettuce, lime, avocado For allergens please see ingredients in bold Our kitchen handles CELERY, SOYA, GLUTEN, TREE NUTS, PEANUTS, MUSTARD, SESAME and SULPHITES. title: Afghan Aubergine (Borani Banjan) With Saucy Spiced Lentils, Garlic Yoghurt, Fresh Mint & Pillowy Flatbreads
method:
new dish Afghan Aubergine (Borani Banjan) With Saucy Spiced Lentils, Garlic Yoghurt, Fresh Mint & Pillowy Flatbreads This dish is inspired by the Afghan recipe, borani banjan, which made with aubergine and yogurt. Crispy roasted aubergine sit over a rich tomato sauce infused with garlic and spices. Although not traditional, we've added lentils to the sauce for extra protein, iron and fibre for gut health. It's all finished with garlic yoghurt, fresh mint and served with our pillowy soft garlic and herb flatbreads for mopping everything up. It's a delicious dish to put in the centre of the table and enjoy feasting style. Prep time 1 Total time 20 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Take out a large frying pan and a saucepan. STEP 2 Trim the aubergine and slice it into 1.5 cm thick rounds. Place your frying pan over medium-high heat and add a generous drizzle of olive oil. Lay the aubergine slices in the pan once the oil is hot, and cook until tender and golden on both sides, about 8–10 minutes. Season both sides with salt and pepper as they cook. STEP 3 Drain the black lentils . Pour the spiced lentil sauce into your saucepan along with the lentils. Place over medium heat and cook until piping hot, around 4-5 minutes. Stir occasionally while cooking, adding a splash of water as needed if it starts to stick. Season with salt to taste. STEP 4 Wipe the frying pan you used for the aubergines and place it over medium-high heat. Warm the flatbreads by dry-frying (without oil) for 1-2 minutes, or until lightly toasted. Alternatively, you can bake or grill them in the oven until warmed through. STEP 5 Pick the mint leaves and roughly slice them. STEP 6 To serve, divide the saucy spiced lentils between serving bowls and lay the sautéed aubergine slices on top. Add dollops of garlic yoghurt , scatter over the sliced mint leaves, and serve with warm garlic & herb flatbreads on the side. Enjoy! What’s in your box Spiced Lentil Sauce Aubergine Garlic Yoghurt Black Lentils Mint Garlic & Herb Flatbread Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. Calories 474kcal Total fat 6.4g Saturated fat 1.3g Salt 1.4g Total carbohydrate 79.2g Total sugar 17.5g Fibre 16.4g Protein 20.2g Ingredients / Allergens Organic black lentils, aubergine, tomato, red onion, garlic, balsamic vinegar ( sulphites ), gluten free low sodium soy sauce, paprika, cumin, coriander, fennel, chilli, raw organic agave nectar, cinnamon, allspice, tomato puree, sea salt, organic cultured soy yoghurt ( soy beans, water, selected live cultures (Bifidus, Acidophilus), lemon, mint, garlic & herb flatbread (wheat flour ( gluten ), salt, yeast, vegetable oil, garlic, mixed herbs) For allergens please see ingredients in bold Please be advised that we handle the following allergens in our kitchen: CELERY, SOYA, GLUTEN, NUTS, PEANUTS, MUSTARD, SESAME SEEDS, SULPHITES

title: Aromatic Thai Green Curry Soup With Beluga Lentils, Tender Aubergine, Sweet Potato, Basil & Lime
method:
gut health Aromatic Thai Green Curry Soup With Beluga Lentils, Tender Aubergine, Sweet Potato, Basil & Lime Our super green Thai curry soup is infused with punchy aromatic flavours like kaffir lime leaves, lemongrass, galangal and garlic with a few subtle spices, all simmered in decadent coconut milk and finished with fresh basil. At home you'll fill it with roasted aubergine and sweet potato which are both full of vitamins B, C and antioxidants. We've also swapped rice for tender beluga lentils which is great for your gut and is high in protein. Prep time 5 Total time 25 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan and take out a large roasting tray, and a large saucepan. STEP 2 Dice the sweet potato and aubergine into 1.5cm cubes. Add the sweet potato to one side of your roasting tray and the aubergine to the other. Drizzle generously with olive oil, season with salt and pepper and roast until tender, around 20-30 minutes. STEP 3 Once there's 5 minutes left on the vegetables, add the green curry soup to your saucepan. Bring to a gentle simmer and cook for a further 2-3 minutes or until piping hot. Meanwhile drain and rinse the lentils well. Roll up the basil leaves with your hands and finely slice them. Set aside a quarter of the basil leaves for a garnish. Add the drained lentils and the remaining three quarters of basil to the soup and cook for a further 2 minutes. Remove from the heat, squeeze in half of the lime and season to taste with salt. STEP 4 To serve, divide the thai green curry soup and lentils between serving bowls, arrange the sweet potato and aubergine around the bowl and garnish with the remaining basil leaves. Squeeze over the remaining lime and enjoy! What’s in your box Green Curry Soup Sweet Potato Basil Black Lentils Aubergine Lime Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Rich Ragu & Roasted Roots Gratin, Topped With Crispy Potato, Beetroot & Creamy Miso Bechamel
method:
new dish Rich Ragu & Roasted Roots Gratin, Topped With Crispy Potato, Beetroot & Creamy Miso Bechamel A rich, hearty, warming dish that's brings together the best of a gratin and a shepherds pie. Featuring a rich lentil ragu, topped with sliced potato and beetroot, and a final layer of creamy miso bechamel. There's minimal prep and it's all roasted in one dish so it's super simple after a long day. When it comes out of the oven the ragu is bubbling, the roasted root veg is crispy and the creamy bechamel delivers a heavenly 'cheesy' flavour.. A seriously delicious way to hit your veg, protein, fibre and iron goals. Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 240°C / 220°C fan. Take out a wide, hob- and ovenproof casserole dish. STEP 2 Slice the cooked beetroot and sweet potato as thinly as possible, about 2mm thick. Use a mandoline if you have one to ensure even thickness. If you struggle to cut thin slices, you can microwave the sweet potato slices (3 minutes on high, covered) or parboil them in boiling water for 3 minutes to ensure they are cooked through when serving. Alternatively, you can extend the oven cooking time (see Step 4). STEP 3 In your ovenproof casserole dish, mix together the roots gratin ragu and the tin of black lentils , including the liquid from the tin. Place over medium heat and gently bring to a simmer, then turn off the heat. STEP 4 Lay the beetroot and sweet potato slices over the lentil ragu, slightly submerging them in the sauce, then drizzle over olive oil. Cover the dish with a lid or a sheet of kitchen foil and bake in the oven for 20 minutes. If the vegetables are cut into thicker slices and the microwaving or parboiling step was skipped, bake in the oven for 35 minutes instead. Once it has cooked for the above time, remove the casserole dish from the oven and pour the miso bechamel over the top. Return it to the oven and bake for a further 10 minutes without a cover, until the sauce is golden on top. STEP 5 To serve, divide the gratin between dishes. Finish with a sprinkle of salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste. Enjoy! What’s in your box Roots Gratin Ragu Miso Bechamel Black Lentils Cooked Beetroot Sweet Potato Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: 24 Plant Frijoles Negros With Caramelised Plantain, Whipped Feta, Pink Pickled Onions, Avocado & Coriander Lime Brown Rice
method:
24 Plant Frijoles Negros With Caramelised Plantain, Whipped Feta, Pink Pickled Onions, Avocado & Coriander Lime Brown Rice Frijoles negros are foundational dish in Latin American and Mexican cuisine, featuring silky smooth black beans simmered with aromatics and spices until tender and creamy. At home you'll serve them fully loaded with caramelised plantain, whipped feta, pink pickled onions and avocado, alongside a side of lime and coriander wholegrain brown rice. Prep time 2 Total time 20 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. Notes on plantain: You may notice dark spots and areas on the plantain skin. This is completely normal for a ripe plantain and makes it perfect for this recipe. STEP 1 Boil water in your kettle, and take out two large saucepans and a frying pan. STEP 2 Add boiling water from the kettle to one of the saucepans, place it over medium-high heat, and bring it to a rolling boil. Add the brown basmati rice and boil uncovered for 18-20 minutes, until the rice is tender with a slight bite, then drain using a fine sieve. Return the rice to the pan, cover with a lid, and let it steam until the meal is ready. STEP 3 Place the other saucepan over a medium heat and add the frijole sauce and the whole tin of black beans . It is important to include the liquid from the tin, as it will add creaminess and a deep colour to the sauce. Bring to a simmer and cook for a further 5 minutes, until the sauce is slightly thickened. STEP 4 Peel, destone, and slice the avocado , then season with the juice of half a lime , salt, and pepper. Slice the remaining half of the lime into wedges. Finely chop half of the coriander including the stalks and pick the rest of the leaves for garnish. STEP 5 Place your frying pan over a medium heat and add a drizzle of olive oil. Peel and slice the plantain into 2cm-thick pieces, then add to the pan and fry on both sides until golden, about 2–3 minutes per side. STEP 6 Before serving, fluff up the brown basmati rice with a fork. Stir in a squeeze of lime juice, finely chopped coriander in the rice and season with salt and pepper to taste. STEP 7 Divide the rice between serving bowls and ladle over the frijole sauce . Add the sautéed plantain , sliced avocado , and pink pickled onions on the side, along with a dollop of feta drizzle . Finish with a scattering of fresh coriander leaves and a squeeze of fresh lime . Enjoy! title: Black Bean Smash Burger Tacos With Homemade Burger Sauce & Cabbage Carrot Apple Walnut Slaw
method:
Black Bean Smash Burger Tacos With Homemade Burger Sauce & Cabbage Carrot Apple Walnut Slaw We have perfected the ultimate smash burger recipe - meaty, juicy, umami-rich - which works perfectly in a crispy folded taco, filled with a fresh, nutrient rich slaw of red cabbage, apple, carrot and walnut, and a house made 'burger sauce'. Tastes like a cheat meal but is the total opposite - high protein, high fibre and full of whole food ingredients like vegetables, beans, nuts and seeds. Prep time 8 Total time 10 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Take out a large frying pan and two mixing bowls. STEP 2 To prepare the slaw, cut and remove the core of the apple . Top and tail the carrot (peel if you prefer), and remove the hard core of the red cabbage . Julienne all of them into uniform, thin shreds (use a mandoline if you have one). Place them in one of the mixing bowls and season with the juice of half a lime , a drizzle of olive oil, and salt and pepper to taste. Roughly chop the walnuts , fold them in, and set aside. Cut the remaining lime into wedges for garnish and set aside. STEP 3 To prepare the burger mixture, place your frying pan over medium-low heat and dry toast (without oil) the gram flour for 2 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it becomes golden. Drain the black beans and transfer them into the other mixing bowl. Roughly mash them with the back of a fork. Add the toasted gram flour and the patty flavour bomb , then stir well to combine. STEP 4 Wipe clean the frying pan you used for toasting the gram flour and place it over medium heat and add a drizzle of olive oil. While the oil heats up, divide the burger mixture and spread it on each of the flour tortillas in an even layer around 2-3mm thick. Place them on the pan with the burger mixture side down and press with a spatula and cook for 4-5 minutes until the mixture starts to caramelise. Flip it onto the other side and cook further 30-60 seconds until the tortilla is golden. Cook in a few batches depending on the size of your frying pan, adding more oil for each batch. STEP 5 To serve, add a dollop of the burger sauce on each of the burger tortillas and fold them in half. Plate them up along with the slaw and lime wedges. You can either stuff the inside of the tortillas with the slaw or place it on top of the folded tacos. Enjoy! What’s in your box Patty Flavour Bomb Black Beans Gram Flour Burger Sauce Flour Tortilla Red Cabbage Carrot Walnuts Apple Lime Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Vietnamese Bún Chay Noodle Salad With Walnut Miso & Mushrooms, Pickled Mooli & Carrot & Fresh Herbs
method:
new dish Vietnamese Bún Chay Noodle Salad With Walnut Miso & Mushrooms, Pickled Mooli & Carrot & Fresh Herbs A light and vibrant Vietnamese vermicelli noodle salad with crispy miso marinated mushrooms and walnuts, tart pickled carrot and mooli and toasted peanuts. Freshened up with mint and coriander, and a zingy lime dressing. Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions A light and vibrant Vietnamese vermicelli noodle salad with crispy miso marinated mushrooms and walnuts, tart pickled carrot and mooli and toasted peanuts. Freshened up with mint and coriander, and a zingy lime dressing. title: Burnt Lemon & Mustard Butter Beans With Regenerative Spelt Conchigliette, Confit Cherry Tomatoes, Cavolo Nero & Dill
method:
Burnt Lemon & Mustard Butter Beans With Regenerative Spelt Conchigliette, Confit Cherry Tomatoes, Cavolo Nero & Dill Tender butter beans simmered in a creamy lemon and mustard sauce with wilted dark leafy greens. Filled with tender spelt conchigiletti made with grains from sustainable, British farms, using regenerative farming practices to improve biodiversity, soil health and nutrient density. Finished with sweet and tangy confit cherry tomatoes and a scattering of fresh dill. Prep time 1 Total time 15 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 200°C / 180°C fan. Boil your kettle and take out an ovenproof ramekin (in a size that snugly fits all the cherry tomatoes in a single layer), a griddle pan or frying pan, as well as two saucepans. STEP 2 Place your cherry tomatoes in the ramekin and pour in good olive oil so the tomatoes are at least half submerged in the oil. Add a sprinkle of salt and pepper, cover tightly with a lid or foil, and place in the oven until serving. You can use less oil, but this is the technique for 'confit' cherry tomatoes and creates a flavoursome finishing oil too. STEP 3 Fill one of your saucepans with the boiling water from the kettle, add salt, and place over medium heat. Cook the spelt conchiglietti until al dente, about 10-12 minutes. STEP 4 Place the other saucepan over medium heat and add the creamy butter bean sauce . Drain the butter beans and add them to the sauce to gently warm through. You can adjust the consistency of the sauce to make it more brothy if you prefer, using pasta cooking water or some of the liquid from the butter bean tin. STEP 5 Halve the lemon and remove the hardy stems from the cavolo nero , then tear the leaves into bite-size pieces. Pick the dill leaves for garnish and set aside. Place your griddle pan or frying pan over high heat and add a drizzle of olive oil. Once hot, place the lemon halves cut-side down and cook until charred and caramelised, around 3-5 minutes. Remove from the pan and set aside. Wipe the same pan clean with kitchen towel, add another drizzle of olive oil, then add the cavolo nero and sauté until vibrant green and crispy at the edges, about 4–5 minutes. STEP 5 Once the pasta is cooked, drain well and add it to the creamy butter bean sauce along with the sautéed cavolo nero and a squeeze from one half of the burnt lemon , then stir to combine. STEP 5 To serve, divide between serving bowls and top with the confit cherry tomatoes . Garnish with dill leaves , drizzle over some of the confit tomato oil , and season with salt and freshly ground black pepper. Squeeze over more burnt lemon juice to taste. Enjoy! What’s in your box Creamy Butter Bean Sauce Cherry Tomatoes on the Vine Spelt Conchiglietti Cavolo Nero Dill Butter Beans Lemon Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Butter Bean Caponata With Roasted Peppers & Aubergine, Salsa Verde & Warm Ciabatta
method:
gut health Butter Bean Caponata With Roasted Peppers & Aubergine, Salsa Verde & Warm Ciabatta The secret to our caponata sauce is that it's made from hand-chopped fresh tomatoes, which add an amazingly rich, yet fresh tasting sauce. We cook it low and slow with garlic and capers until rich and flavoursome, then stir it through roasted peppers and aubergines and protein-packed butterbeans. Served with a zingy salsa verde and a freshly baked organic toasted ciabatta for dunking in. Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions The secret to our caponata sauce is that it's made from hand-chopped fresh tomatoes, which add an amazingly rich, yet fresh tasting sauce. We cook it low and slow with garlic and capers until rich and flavoursome, then stir it through roasted peppers and aubergines and protein-packed butterbeans. Served with a zingy salsa verde and a freshly baked organic toasted ciabatta for dunking in. title: Butternut Squash Brothy Beans With Charred Courgette, Crispy Sage, Hot Agave 'Nduja' Dressing, Toasted Pine Nuts & Pumpkin Seeds
method:
gut health Butternut Squash Brothy Beans With Charred Courgette, Crispy Sage, Hot Agave 'Nduja' Dressing, Toasted Pine Nuts & Pumpkin Seeds Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan and take out a large saucepan, a frying pan and a baking tray. STEP 2 Cut the courgette into rounds or batons around 0.5cm thick. Place the courgettes onto your baking tray, toss in a little olive oil and season well with salt and pepper. Roast in the oven until brown and crisp, around 15-20 minutes, turning/tossing halfway. STEP 3 Place a saucepan over medium–high heat and add the squash broth along with the butter beans , including the liquid from the tin. Once the sauce begins to bubble, reduce the heat to low and let it simmer gently until serving. Add a splash of water as needed to reach your preferred consistency. STEP 4 Place your frying pan over a low-medium heat, then dry fry (without oil) the pumpkin and pine nuts until lightly toasted. Remove from the pan and set aside. Add a few tablespoons of olive oil to the pan and place it back over a medium heat. When hot, add the sage leaves without overlapping. The leaves will turn translucent and the oil will stop bubbling around the leaves when they are crisp. Remove and set aside. STEP 5 To serve up, divide the butternut squash brothy beans between serving bowls and top with the courgettes , toasted pumpkin and pine nuts , crispy sage and finish with a drizzle of hot agave nduja . Enjoy! What’s in your box Squash Broth Courgette Hot Agave Nduja Pumpkin & Pine Nuts Sage Butter Beans Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Butternut Squash Brothy Beans With Crispy Sprouts & Sage, Hot Agave 'Nduja' Dressing, Toasted Pine Nuts & Pumpkin Seeds
method:
popular Butternut Squash Brothy Beans With Crispy Sprouts & Sage, Hot Agave 'Nduja' Dressing, Toasted Pine Nuts & Pumpkin Seeds This might just be the best recipes we've tasted coming out of our test kitchen. The broth is the star of the show, featuring a rich onion, garlic and white wine broth, stirred through with sweet and nutty pureed butternut squash. At home you'll simmer tender butterbeans in the delicious broth, then top with crispy roasted sprouts, a drizzle of hot agave 'nduja' oil to cut through the richness and a sprinkle of toasted pine nuts and pumpkin seeds for a final hit of nutrition and flavour. 20g of fibre and 20g of protein make this dish as nutritious as it is delicious. Prep time 5 Total time 20 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan and take out a large saucepan, a frying pan and a baking tray. STEP 2 Trim the bottoms of the brussels sprouts if needed, then slice them thinly with a sharp knife. Alternatively you can cut them in half but thinly slicing will make them crispier and quicker to cook. Place the sprouts onto your baking tray, toss in a little olive oil and season well with salt and pepper. Roast in the oven until brown and crisp, around 15-20 minutes, turning/tossing halfway. STEP 3 Add the butter beans along with its brine in the tin to your saucepan along with the squash broth . Once there is 5 minutes left of cooking time on the sprouts, place the pan over a medium-high heat. Once the sauce is bubbling, reduce to low-medium and cook for 2-3 minutes or until piping hot. STEP 4 Place your frying pan over a low-medium heat, then dry fry (without oil) the pumpkin and pine nuts until lightly toasted. Remove from the pan and set aside. Add a few tablespoons of olive oil to the pan and place it back over a medium heat. When hot, add the sage leaves without overlapping. The leaves will turn translucent and the oil will stop bubbling around the leaves when they are crisp. Remove and set aside. STEP 5 To serve up, divide the butternut squash brothy beans between serving bowls and top with the crispy shredded sprouts , toasted pumpkin and pine nuts , crispy sage and finish with a drizzle of hot agave nduja . Enjoy! What’s in your box Squash Broth Brussels Sprouts Pumpkin & Pine Nuts Hot Agave Nduja Sage Butterbeans Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Butternut Squash Curried Noodles With Tenderstem Broccoli & Superseed Chilli Rayu Drizzle
method:
Butternut Squash Curried Noodles With Tenderstem Broccoli & Superseed Chilli Rayu Drizzle Chewy udon noodles in our silky, rich, aromatic curried coconut sauce, topped with roasted butternut squash, vitamin-rich tenderstem broccoli and finished with our sweet and smoky superseed chilli rayu drizzle. Prep time 2 Total time 15 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan. Take out a baking tray and a saucepan. STEP 2 Halve the butternut squash and remove the seeds using a spoon. Cut each half into wedges about 2 cm thick. Place them on the baking tray, drizzle with olive oil, and season with salt and pepper. Roast in the oven for 20 minutes, or until the edges start to caramelise. You may receive a particularly large butternut squash depending on the season. Around 200 g of squash flesh per person works well for this recipe, so you can save the rest for another meal. Leftover roasted butternut squash will keep in an airtight container in the refrigerator for 3 to 5 days. STEP 3 Meanwhile, fill a saucepan with boiling water from the kettle and bring it back to the boil. Cook the tenderstem broccoli until vibrant green and al dente, about 3 to 4 minutes, then remove using a slotted spoon. Add the flat rice noodles to the same water and cook for 5 minutes, or until al dente. Drain and rinse well under cold running water, then leave in a colander to drain. Quickly rinse the saucepan ready for the next step. STEP 4 Place the saucepan back over medium heat and add the curried coconut broth along with 120 ml of water (double the amount for four portions). Gently heat until piping hot. STEP 5 Pick the coriander leaves and halve the lime , ready for garnish. STEP 6 To serve, divide the flat rice noodles between serving bowls and pour over the curried coconut broth . Top with the roasted butternut squash wedges and tenderstem broccoli , then drizzle over the superseed chilli crunch . Finish with a scattering of coriander leaves and a squeeze of fresh lime . Enjoy! What’s in your box Curried Coconut Broth Butternut Squash Superseed Chilli Crunch Tenderstem Broccoli Flat Rice Noodles Coriander Lime Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Smoky Butternut Squash & "Nduja" Lasagne With Tender Lentils & Creamy Miso Spinach Bechamel
method:
high protein Smoky Butternut Squash & "Nduja" Lasagne With Tender Lentils & Creamy Miso Spinach Bechamel Our healthy spin on a lasagne, packed with 35g of protein and 30g of fibre. Featuring layers of smoky squash and 'nduja' lentils, and silky miso bechamel with baby spinach. The sauces are all made by our lovely chefs for you, so all you need to do is assemble and whack it in the oven! Prep time 5 Total time 30 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 200°C / 180°C fan. Take out a saucepan, a mixing bowl and a baking dish around 15x20cm (or double this for a four person box). STEP 2 Drain the lentils and add them to your mixing bowl along with the squash & nduja filling . Give everything a good mix to combine. STEP 3 Place a saucepan over medium heat and add the creamy miso bechamel . Bring it to a gentle simmer, then add the baby spinach and cook for about a minute until wilted. STEP 4 Build your lasagne in three layers: Spread one-third of the lentils, squash & nduja mix in the base of your dish. Cover with lasag ne sheets (about 2½ sheets per layer). Top with one-third of the baby spinach & miso b echamel , then another third of the lentils, squash & nduja mix . Repeat with more lasagne sheets , another third of baby spinach & miso b echamel , and the remaining lentils, squash & nduja mix . Finish with a final layer of lasagne sheets and the rest of the baby spinach & miso b echamel . Drizzle with olive oil, season with salt and pepper, then bake for 25–30 minutes, until bubbling and golden. To check doneness, insert a knife into the centre, and if it’s hot through and the pasta is tender, it's ready. STEP 5 Place the lasagne in the middle of the table and divide it between serving plates. We like stacking pieces on top of each other to make one bigger slice per person, but you could serve them as they are too. Enjoy! What’s in your box Squash & Nduja Filling Miso Bechamel Baby Spinach Lasagne Sheets Organic Brown Lentils Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Butternut Squash & Spelt Risotto With Crispy Sage & Smoky Toasted Pine Nuts
method:
popular Butternut Squash & Spelt Risotto With Crispy Sage & Smoky Toasted Pine Nuts This autumnal squash dish is inspired by risotto, but made so much healthier by swapping out the traditional arborio rice for the nutritionally superior spelt. It’s a nutty, chewy grain that works so well with the super creamy miso butternut squash sauce. Finished off with a smoky pine nut drizzle and crispy sage. Prep time 8 Total time 25 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 Boil your kettle, then take out a large saucepan, a large frying pan, and a small frying pan. STEP 2 Pour the boiling water from your kettle into the large saucepan and salt the water. Place over medium-high heat. Once the water is bubbling, add the pearled spelt and reduce to a simmer. Cook until al dente, around 20 minutes. STEP 3 Meanwhile remove the hardy stems from the cavolo nero and cut the leaves into bite-sized pieces. Once the spelt is 5 minutes from being ready, place your large frying pan over medium heat, add a drizzle of olive oil, and sauté the cavolo nero until tender and a little crispy at the edges, around 5 minutes. Zest half of the lemon over the cavolo nero and season with salt and pepper. Cut the lemon into wedges for garnish. STEP 4 Once the pearled spelt is cooked al dente, reserve a mug of the cooking water, then drain the spelt. Return it to the saucepan and add the butternut squash sauce . Place the saucepan back over medium heat, stirring occasionally, and cook until the sauce is bubbling. Use the reserved spelt cooking water to adjust the consistency until creamy. STEP 5 Heat a few tablespoons of olive oil in your small frying pan. When hot, pick the sage leaves and add to the oil in a single layer without overlapping. The leaves will turn translucent and the oil will stop bubbling around them when they’re crisp. Turn off the heat and sprinkle in the smoky pine nut mix so it toasts slightly without burning. STEP 6 To serve, divide the risotto between serving bowls. Scatter over the crispy sage and smoky pine nuts , then drizzle with any remaining sage oil. Squeeze over the lemon juice to taste and season with salt and pepper. Serve with a side of zesty sautéed cavolo nero and enjoy! What’s in your box Butternut Squash Sauce Smoky Pine Nut Mix Cavolo Nero Sage Spelt Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Butternut Squash & Sage Stuffed Conchiglioni Pasta Shells In Creamy Garlic & Spinach Sauce
method:
Butternut Squash & Sage Stuffed Conchiglioni Pasta Shells In Creamy Garlic & Spinach Sauce This family friendly dish delivers big on comfort, flavour and nutrition and is fun and easy to finish at home. Featuring conchiglioni pasta (giant shells), stuffed with roasted butternut squash and sage, layered into a rich and creamy garlic and spinach sauce. After baking you get a mixture of tender filled pasta shells and crispy edges for an incredibly delicious, satisfying meal. Prep time 5 Total time 20 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan. Boil your kettle and take out a large saucepan and a wide ovenproof or casserole dish. STEP 2 Place the saucepan over medium heat and pour in the boiling water from the kettle. Salt the water generously, add the conchiglioni pasta shells , and cook for 8 minutes, or until al dente. Take care not to overcook, as the pasta should remain slightly firm. This will make it easier to fill with the squash puree and it will continue cooking in the oven later. Drain well and rinse the pasta so it is cool enough to handle for filling. STEP 3 Pour the creamy gratin sauce into your ovenproof or casserole dish. Fold in the baby spinach and spread the mixture evenly across the base of the dish. Fill each conchiglioni shell with the squash puree using a dessert spoon, then arrange them in a single layer on top of the gratin sauce. Gently press them into the sauce so they are half submerged. Drizzle over good-quality olive oil and bake for 10–15 minutes, or until the pasta is tender and slightly crispy in places, and the squash filling is lightly golden. STEP 4 To serve, divide the butternut squash & sage stuffed conchiglioni with the creamy sauce between serving plates. Finish with salt and freshly ground black pepper. Enjoy! What’s in your box Squash Puree Creamy Gratin Sauce Giant Conchiglioni Baby Spinach Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Caprese Salad With 'Burrata', Hazelnut Pesto, Rustic Croutons & Tender Butterbeans
method:
summer special Caprese Salad With 'Burrata', Hazelnut Pesto, Rustic Croutons & Tender Butterbeans We've taken the classic Italian Caprese salad and amped it up with layers of textures and flavours. The star of the show is our new creamy vegan 'burrata' which is unbelievably reminiscent of its dairy counterpart, but made from soy! Served over juicy vine ripened plum tomatoes with golden rustic croutons, sharp red onion, plump butterbeans and finished with a zingy hazelnut pesto - this is summer in a bowl. Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 200°C and take out a roasting tray. STEP 2 Cut each tomato into eight pieces by quartering them and then cutting each quarter in half. Peel and finely slice half of the red onion, saving the other half for another recipe. Open, drain and rinse the butterbeans . Add the tomatoes , butterbeans and sliced onion to a large mixing bowl, toss in a healthy glug of extra virgin olive oil and season well with salt and pepper. Leave to sit while you prepare the croutons, so the tomatoes release their juices. STEP 3 Tear the ciabatta into rough chunks and place in your roasting tray. Toss the croutons in a drizzle of olive oil and season well with salt and pepper. Roast for 10 minutes or until golden. Once they’re done, mix 2/3rds of them through the tomatoes in the mixing bowl. STEP 4 Divide the dressed tomatoes between serving bowls and scatter over the remaining ciabatta croutons . Add a spoon of vegan 'burrata' to the middle of the bowl, then drizzle the hazelnut pesto over everything (if the pesto has thickened in the fridge you can loosen the pesto with a little olive oil or water). Season to taste with salt or pepper and enjoy! What’s in your box Vegan 'Burrata' Vine Plum Tomatoes Hazelnut Pesto Organic Ciabatta Red Onion Butter Beans Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Crispy Caramelised Brussels Sprouts & Parsnips With Farro, Tangy Caper Salsa Verde & Toasted Hazelnuts
method:
Crispy Caramelised Brussels Sprouts & Parsnips With Farro, Tangy Caper Salsa Verde & Toasted Hazelnuts Treat your body to a delicious, protein and fibre-rich medley of caramelised brussels sprouts and parsnips, tossed through nutrient-rich farro grains and smothered in an addictive, tangy caper salsa verde made from rocket, mint and parsley. Finished with a scattering of crushed toasted hazelnuts. Prep time 4 Total time 30 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 This is one of very few recipes that takes a little longer than 15 minutes, but we hope you'll forgive us when you see how easy it is to finish! STEP 2 Boil your kettle, pre-heat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan, then take out a large roasting tray and a saucepan. STEP 3 Slice off and discard the bottom end of the brussels sprouts , then slice vertically in half. Cut the parsnip into 1.5cm cubes. Add the parsnips to your roasting tray, toss in a drizzle of olive oil, season well with salt and pepper and roast for 10 minutes. Then add the brussels sprouts tossed in oil, salt and pepper and roast for a further 15-20 minutes or until golden and caramelised. Add the hazelnuts to one side of your baking tray for the final 5 minutes of cooking time. STEP 4 Meanwhile, pour boiling water from your kettle into your saucepan and place over a medium heat. Add the farro and boil for 15 minute or until tender but al dente. STEP 5 To serve up, divide the brussels sprouts , parsnips and farro between serving plates, scatter over the toasted hazelnuts , then smother everything generously in tangy caper salsa verde dressing. Enjoy! What’s in your box Salsa Verde Farro Brussels Sprouts Hazelnuts Parsnip Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Comforting Slow-Cooked Caribbean Jackfruit Curry With Coconut Rice & Braised Cavolo Nero
method:

Served with fluffy coconut rice and vitamin-rich cavolo nero. Cooking Mode:

Served with fluffy coconut rice and vitamin-rich cavolo nero. title: Crispy Cauliflower Al Pastor Tacos With Jalapeño Salsa, Chipotle Cashew Crema, Pickled Cabbage & Lime
method:
new dish Crispy Cauliflower Al Pastor Tacos With Jalapeño Salsa, Chipotle Cashew Crema, Pickled Cabbage & Lime Crispy cauliflower bites marinated in a smokey guajillo adobo sauce, served in soft corn tortillas with tangy pickled cabbage, fiery jalapeno salsa and smokey chipotle cashew crema. Prep time 1 Total time 15 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 200°C / 220°C fan, then take out a large roasting tray and a frying pan. STEP 2 Cut the cauliflower into bite-sized chunks, place them in the roasting tray, and toss with a drizzle of olive oil and the adobo sauce , ensuring each piece is evenly coated. Roast in the oven for 15–20 minutes, or until the sauce starts to caramelise. STEP 3 Place your frying pan over a medium heat and dry-fry the corn tacos (without oil) for 30 seconds on each side, or until warm and soft. Add a sprinkle of water if you’d like them extra soft. Alternatively, place the stack of tacos on a plate and cover with a damp kitchen towel. Microwave on medium for 30 seconds at a time; after about a minute, the tacos should be soft and fragrant. STEP 4 Cut the lime into quarters and drain the red wine vinegar pickled red cabbage . STEP 5 To serve, lay the tacos on a large plate and top with the pickled red cabbage and roasted cauliflower . Drizzle over the jalapeno salsa and cashew crema , then finish with a squeeze of fresh lime juice. Enjoy! What’s in your box Adobo Sauce Cauliflower Florets Jalapeno Salsa Cashew Crema Pickled Red Cabbage Corn Tortillas Lime Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Charred Aubergine With Romesco, Bulgar Wheat Pilaf, Tahini Sauce & Herby Caper Gremolata
method:
gut health Charred Aubergine With Romesco, Bulgar Wheat Pilaf, Tahini Sauce & Herby Caper Gremolata Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan and take out a large roasting tray, and a saucepan. STEP 2 Slice the aubergine into rounds, about 1cm thick. We recommend roasting the aubergine for better flavour, but it does take longer. If you want the meal ready within 15 minutes, simply fry the aubergine with a healthy drizzle of olive oil in a medium-hot frying pan for 5-7 minutes on each side until golden and tender. Otherwise, if you're happy to wait 30 mins, lay the aubergine rounds on a baking tray, brush with olive oil on both sides, and season with salt and pepper. Roast for 15 mins, then flip the aubergines and roast for another 10-15 mins until softened and golden. Alternatively, if you have an air fryer, you could air fry them for 15 minutes, flipping halfway. You could also turn the chickpeas into crispy chickpeas by draining, patting dry and then roasting or air frying the chickpeas in oil, salt and pepper for the same amount of time as the aubergine (optional, but recommended!). STEP 3 Place the bulgur wheat in a saucepan and cover with cold water. Bring to a boil and then turn down to a simmer and cook until tender but al dente, around 6-8 minutes. If you haven't roasted the chickpeas, simply drain and add these to the bulgur wheat pan for the final one minute of cooking time. Stir 2 tbsp of the herby caper gremolata through the bulgur wheat and season well with salt and pepper. This is your bulgur and chickpea pilaf. STEP 4 To serve, spread half of the romesco sauce (cold) onto the bottom of each plate, top with the bulgur pilaf , then with the aubergine and finish with the tahini drizzle and remaining herby caper gremolata . If you made crispy chickpeas, scatter these over at the end. Enjoy! What’s in your box Romesco Sauce Aubergine Herby Caper Gremolata Bulgar Wheat Tahini Drizzle Chickpeas Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Charred Courgettes With Romesco, Chickpeas, Giant Cous Cous, Tahini Sauce & Mint Pesto
method:
new dish Charred Courgettes With Romesco, Chickpeas, Giant Cous Cous, Tahini Sauce & Mint Pesto Romesco is the star of the show in this dish and the ultimate summer sauce, made with roasted red peppers, almonds and a zingy drizzle of premium sherry vinegar. We're serving it with tender grilled courgettes, fibre and protein packed chickpeas, giant cous cous, then finishing with garlic tahini sauce and a vibrant minty pesto. Delicious! Prep time 2 Total time 15 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Boil your kettle, pre-heat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan and take out a large roasting tray, a small frying pan and a saucepan. STEP 2 Place your frying pan over a medium heat and add the almonds . Dry fry (without oil) for 3-4 minutes or until nicely toasted, tossing occasionally so they don't burn. Set aside and roughly chop or crush the almonds by pressing down on them with a rolling pin or bottom of a heavy pan. STEP 3 Cut the courgette in half lengthways, then into large, diagonal pieces. Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt and pepper, then place on your roasting tray. Cook for 15 minutes or until the courgette is caramelised and starting to char. STEP 4 Fill your saucepan with boiling water and place over a high heat. Once it's bubbling, reduce to a simmer and add the giant cous cous . Cook until tender but al dente, around 6-8 minutes. Drain the chickpeas then add them to the pan for the final one minute of cooking time. STEP 5 Drain the giant cous cous and chickpeas . Add them to a bowl along with the cooked courgettes and mix through half of the mint pesto . STEP 6 To serve, spread the romesco sauce (cold) at the bottom of a plate and top with the chickpeas , couscous and courgettes . Drizzle over the remaining mint pesto and tahini sauce , and sprinkle the toasted almonds to finish. Enjoy! What’s in your box Romesco Sauce Mint Pesto Giant Couscous Courgette Tahini Drizzle Chickpeas Almonds Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Charred Gochujang Glazed Hispi Cabbage Over Lime Tahini Yoghurt With Lentils & Orange Peanut Crumb
method:
gut health Charred Gochujang Glazed Hispi Cabbage Over Lime Tahini Yoghurt With Lentils & Orange Peanut Crumb Prep time 3 Total time 20 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 Pre-heat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan, then take out a large frying pan and two baking trays. STEP 2 Place your frying pan on the hob over a high heat. Cut the hispi cabbage in half length-ways, then again length-ways into quarters. Rub each quarter with olive oil and a little salt and pepper. Once your pan is searing hot, char the cabbage cut side down, until each of the two cut sides of the wedges are starting to blacken a little, around 5 minutes. STEP 3 Drain the lentils and transfer them to one of your baking trays. Season with a little drizzle of olive oil, salt and pepper, then spread them evenly in a thin layer. Place the charred cabbage wedges on the other baking tray, cut side up. Put both trays in the oven and roast for 15 minutes. About halfway through, remove the cabbage wedges and brush the cut sides with the gochujang sauce . Towards the end of the cooking time, you may want to baste the cabbage a couple of times to ensure the sauce seeps into the layers and caramelises on top. STEP 4 Meanwhile finely chop the chives for garnish. STEP 5 To serve, spoon the lime tahini yoghurt onto two plates and top with the lentils . Place two pieces of hispi cabbage wedges onto each plate and drizzle with any leftover gochujang sauce from the baking tray . Finish with the chopped chives and orange peanut crumb . Enjoy! What’s in your box Gochujang Sauce Hispi Cabbage Black Lentils Lime Tahini Yoghurt Orange Peanut Crumb Chives Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Charred Hispi Cabbage Over Golden Daal With Organic Soy Yoghurt, Tamarind & Date Chutney, Toasted Almonds & Coriander
method:
high protein Charred Hispi Cabbage Over Golden Daal With Organic Soy Yoghurt, Tamarind & Date Chutney, Toasted Almonds & Coriander Deeply fragrant with smoky, charred edges, our tamarind glazed hispi cabbage is served over our best-selling creamy golden daal, featuring protein-and-fibre-packed red lentils simmered in coconut milk with turmeric, cardamom, ginger, garlic and garam masala. At home you'll garnish the dish with a delicious combination of cooling organic cultured soy yoghurt, sweet and sour tamarind date chutney and a scattering of toasted flaked almonds, fresh coriander and red chilli. This is a truly decadent, restaurant quality dish that's low in carbs and high in protein (over 30g) and fibre (over 20g). Prep time 2 Total time 20 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan and take out a saucepan and an ovenproof skillet - if you don't have an ovenproof one, take out a frying pan and baking tray instead. STEP 2 Place your skillet or frying pan over a high heat. Add the flaked almonds and dry fry (without oil) until toasted. Set aside and wipe the pan clean with some kitchen towel. STEP 3 Meanwhile, cut the hispi cabbage into quarters lengthways and rub with a generous amount of olive oil. Season well with salt and pepper. Using the same pan you toasted the almonds in, place the hispi cabbage cut side down and fry until nicely charred on high heat. Turn and repeat on the other cut side. Turn the heat off, then place the cabbage quarters onto their backs so the cut sides are facing upwards. Transfer the pan to the oven and roast until tender in the centre and charred around the edges, about 15 minutes. STEP 4 Add the daal sauce , red lentils and 200ml of water to your other saucepan and cook for 10-15 mins until the lentils are soft and tender. Stir occasionally while cooking and add more water as needed if it reduces too much or starts to stick. Season to taste with salt. STEP 5 Finely slice the red chilli and roughly chop the coriander . STEP 6 To serve up, divide the daal between serving bowls, top with the charred hispi cabbage and drizzle over the date tamarind chutney, soy yoghurt , some chopped coriander and sliced red chilli . Season to taste with salt and pepper and enjoy! Please note: although the dates in the date tamarind chutney are carefully pitted, there is a very small possibility of traces of stones What’s in your box Coconut Daal Sauce Hispi Cabbage Date Tamarind Chutney Organic Soy Yoghurt Coriander Red Lentils Flaked Almonds Red Chilli Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Cheats Aubergine & Lentil Parmigiana With Creamy Miso Bechamel & Seasonal Salad Tossed In Vinaigrette
method:
new dish Cheats Aubergine & Lentil Parmigiana With Creamy Miso Bechamel & Seasonal Salad Tossed In Vinaigrette This is a super easy way to enjoy all of the flavours you know and love from an aubergine parmigiana, with a gut-health twist by adding fibre-packed lentils. Featuring tender aubergine simmered in a tomato and basil sauce, all finished with our signature creamy miso bechamel for a crispy cheesy finish. Served with a side of seasonal leaves tossed in a classic French vinaigrette - you really can't beat it! Prep time 5 Total time 30 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 Take out a large ovenproof casserole dish and one mixing bowl, and preheat your oven to 200°C /180°C fan. STEP 2 Trim the top and end of the aubergine , then slice it lengthways into 1.5cm thick slices. STEP 3 Place the casserole dish over medium-high heat and add a generous drizzle of olive oil. Once hot, add the aubergine slices, season with salt, and sauté until tender in the centre and lightly golden on both sides, about 4-5 minutes per side. Remove the aubergine from the pan and set aside. STEP 4 Drain the brown lentils and add them to the casserole dish along with the tomato sauce . Place the casserole dish over a medium heat and cook until the sauce is bubbling. Arrange the aubergine slices in the casserole so they are evenly distributed in the sauce. Push them down so they are partly submerged beneath the sauce but with some edges still sitting above the surface, so that they crisp up a little when baked. Pour the miso bechamel over the top, then place the dish into the oven and bake for 15 minutes or until the bechamel has thickened and browned a little on top. STEP 5 In the meantime, toss the mixed leaf salad with the french vinaigrette and season to taste with salt and freshly ground black pepper. STEP 6 To serve, divide the aubergine parmigiana between serving bowls and add a side of mixed leaf salad . Enjoy! What’s in your box Tomato Sauce Miso Bechamel Aubergine Brown Lentils French Vinaigrette Mixed Leaf Salad Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: High Protein Chilli Garlic Tempeh Noodles With Crunchy Rainbow Vegetables & Toasted Sesame Seeds
method:
high protein High Protein Chilli Garlic Tempeh Noodles With Crunchy Rainbow Vegetables & Toasted Sesame Seeds Tender, chewy noodles served with meaty tempeh in a smoky, spicy, umami-rich sauce featuring chilli, garlic, ginger, red miso and five spice. Served with crunchy rainbow vegetables like carrot, pak choi and red cabbage and finished with toasted sesame seeds and freshly sliced spring onions. With almost 40g of protein and 20g of fibre, it's as nutritious as it is delicious! Prep time 8 Total time 15 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 Boil your kettle, then take out a sauce pan and a deep frying pan or wok. STEP 2 Fill your saucepan with boiling water from the kettle and place it over medium heat. Once the water is bubbling, add the udo n noodles and cook for 5-6 minutes, or until al dente. Drain and rinse well under cold running water, then leave them in a colander to drain. STEP 3 Julienne the carrot and finely slice the red cabbage (a mandolin can be helpful if you have one) and pak choi . Finely slice the spring onions , keeping the green and white parts separate. This is all your veg prep done. STEP 4 Crumble the tempeh into small, bite-sized pieces. Place a frying pan or wok over medium-high heat and add a generous drizzle of neutral oil. Fry the crumbled tempeh for 6-8 minutes, until it starts to brown and turn crispy at the edges. Stir in one-third of the chili garlic tempeh sauce and make sure all the tempeh pieces are evenly coated. Set aside and keep warm. STEP 5 Wipe clean or quickly rinse the frying pan or wok used for the tempeh if there are any burnt bits at the bottom. Place it back over medium-high heat and add another drizzle of neutral oil. Add the prepared carrot, red cabbage , pak choi , and the white part of the spring onions , then stir-fry for 5 minutes. Add the drained udon noodles along with the remaining chili garlic tempeh sauce , tossing everything to make sure it's well combined and evenly coated in the sauce. Add a splash of water if needed to prevent burning or sticking, and season to taste with salt or a splash of soy sauce (optional). STEP 5 To serve, divide the noodles between serving bowls, top with the tempeh and then garnish with the green spring onions and a sprinkle of sesame garnish . Enjoy! title: Charred Cauliflower Steaks With Chimichurri, Pine Nuts & Pomegranate Seeds Over Creamy Butterbean Mash
method:
high protein Charred Cauliflower Steaks With Chimichurri, Pine Nuts & Pomegranate Seeds Over Creamy Butterbean Mash Our sweet and smokey cauliflower steaks are loaded with layers upon layers of flavour. Tossed in a sweet and smokey marinade, then charred and smothered in tahini sauce and a punchy chimichurri dressing. Served over a creamy butterbean puree and finished with a scattering of toasted pine nuts and pomegranate seeds. Prep time 8 Total time 25 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan and take out a large roasting tray and a small saucepan. STEP 2 Remove any tough or damaged outer leaves from the cauliflower . Using a large knife, trim the base and cut the cauliflower into quarters, keeping the leaves intact. Rub some olive oil, salt, pepper and drizzle over the smoky cauliflower marinad e . Use your hands or a brush to spread the marinade and seasoning all over the cauliflower. Place on your roasting tray and roast in the oven for 20-30 minutes or until it's browning/blackening on the outside and tender but al dente in the centre. STEP 3 In the meantime, drain the butter beans and add them to a blender with half of the lemon tahini dressing . Blend to a smooth puree (you may need to add a little water if it's too thick). Alternatively if you don't have a blender, you can mash the beans with a fork or potato masher for a chunkier texture. When there's 5 minutes left on the cauliflower, add the tahini butterbean mash to your saucepan and warm through over a medium heat until piping hot, around 3-5 minutes. If it starts to stick, you may need to add water as you cook to loosen. Season generously with salt and pepper to taste. STEP 4 Place your small frying pan over medium heat, and dry toast the pine nuts until golden. STEP 5 To serve, spread the creamy butter bean mash onto a large plate and place the cauliflower steaks on top. Drizzle over the chimichurri dressing and the remaining lemon tahini dressing . Scatter with pomegranate seeds and toasted pine nuts , and finish with a drizzle of good-quality olive oil (optional). Enjoy! title: Chinese Pancakes With Crispy Pulled Oyster Mushrooms, Date Hoi Sin, Fresh Cucumber & Spring Onions
method:
family favourites Chinese Pancakes With Crispy Pulled Oyster Mushrooms, Date Hoi Sin, Fresh Cucumber & Spring Onions Our healthy vegan spin on a Chinese classic. Pulled oyster mushrooms are tossed in cornstarch, fried until deliciously crispy, then piled onto soft pancakes and smothered in a sweet and tangy date hoi sin sauce. Freshened up with crunchy cucumber and shredded spring onion. Prep time 5 Total time 15 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 Take out a large non-stick frying pan and set a steamer over the hob if you don't have a microwave for warming the pancakes. STEP 2 Use your hands to pull apart the oyster mushrooms into thin strips. Place the cornstarch into a large bowl, season well with salt and pepper, then add the oyster mushrooms and give them a good mix so until coated evenly. Place your non-stick frying pan over a medium heat and add a drizzle of oil. Once hot, fry the oyster mushrooms in batches until golden and crispy, around 10-15 minutes. Make sure you fry in batches leaving space between the mushrooms, otherwise they won't crisp up. If they stick together as you cook, break them up regularly, using two forks. Keep frying until they're all golden and crispy on all sides. Season the mushrooms with sea salt to taste. STEP 3 Meanwhile, finely slice the spring onions lengthways (including the green bits!) and cut the cucumber into matchsticks. Julienne the carrot , or you can use a vegetable peeler to make ribbons. Pierce the film packet the pancakes are in, then place in your steamer for 4-5 minutes or microwave on a low heat for 30 seconds. STEP 4 Divide the pancakes between serving plates, then load them up with a hoisin sauce and crispy oyster mushrooms . Finish with a garnish of spring onion , cucumber and carrot . Enjoy! What’s in your box Hoisin Sauce Oyster Mushrooms Chinese Pancake Cornflour Cucumber Spring Onions Carrot Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Chipotle Black Bean Burrito Bowl With Roasted Sweet Potato, Avocado, Salsa Roja, Lime Crema & Tortilla Dippers
method:
gut health Chipotle Black Bean Burrito Bowl With Roasted Sweet Potato, Avocado, Salsa Roja, Lime Crema & Tortilla Dippers Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 Take out two saucepans, a large and a small roasting tray. Preheat your oven to 200°C / 180°C fan. STEP 2 Cut the sweet potato into 1.5cm cubes and add to your large roasting tray. Toss through a drizzle of oil and season well with salt and pepper. Roast for 15-20 minutes or until tender. Alternatively, air fry for 20 minutes, shaking the tray every 5 minutes for an even cook. STEP 3 Place the rice into one of your saucepans and cover with 2.5x its volume in water. Bring to the boil, then reduce the heat, cover and continue to cook for 8-10 minutes or until tender but al dente. Drain and set aside to keep warm. STEP 4 Use scissors or a knife to cut the fresh corn tortillas into triangles, place into your smaller baking tray and drizzle or brush them with a little olive oil. Once the sweet potato has had 5 minutes, bake the corn tortillas for the final 10 minutes of cooking time until perfectly crispy, turning halfway through. Season with a little salt and pepper. STEP 5 Drain the beans and add them into your other saucepan along with the chipotle sauce and 1 tbsp of extra virgin olive oil and warm them through, around 5 minutes. Season well with salt and pepper. STEP 6 Cut the avocado in half and scoop out the flesh. Slice it thinly. STEP 7 To serve, add the rice , beans , sweet potato and avocado into two bowls. Spoon over the salsa roja , lime crema and serve the crispy tortilla chips on the side. title: Courgette Gratin With Cashew Cheese Sauce, Creamy Cannellini Beans And Olive & Almond Gremolata
method:
new dish Courgette Gratin With Cashew Cheese Sauce, Creamy Cannellini Beans And Olive & Almond Gremolata Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan and take out a wide hob- and oven-proof casserole dish or deep ovenproof pan, along with a roasting tray. STEP 2 Drain and rinse the cannellini beans and add half of it along with the creamy gratin sauce to your casserole dish and place over medium low heat and bring to a gentle simmer. Spread the rest of the cannellini beans on a roasting tray and stir through a drizzle of olive oil and season with salt and pepper. STEP 3 Slice both the green courgette and yellow courgette into thin rounds, about 2mm thick. Use a mandoline if you have one to get even slices for the best result. Add about ⅓ to half of them into the simmering gratin sauce, and lay the rest in an even single layer on top. Gently press them into the sauce. Drizzle over some olive oil and place in the oven to bake for 15-20minutes until the top is golden and courgette is cooked. Use a knife to check the doneness before removing from the oven. Place the tray of seasoned cannellini beans into the oven at the same time. They should become golden and crispy in 15-20min. STEP 4 Season the rocket with half of the olive & almond gremolata and - when they're ready - stir through the crispy roasted cannellini beans. STEP 5 To serve, divide the courgette gratin between serving dishes and drizzle over the rest of the olive & almond gremolata . Add the rocket salad to the side and enjoy! title: Creamy Butter Beans With Burnt Lemon & Mustard Sauce, Wilted Greens, Charred Cauliflower & Green Olive Salsa
method:
gut health Creamy Butter Beans With Burnt Lemon & Mustard Sauce, Wilted Greens, Charred Cauliflower & Green Olive Salsa Boosting your gut health has never been tastier than with this creamy beany bowl of deliciousness. Tender butter beans are simmered in a creamy lemon and mustard sauce with wilted dark leafy greens. At home you'll serve it with another portion of fibre-packed veg in the form of charred cauliflower and then smother it in a final flavour bomb of zingy green olive salsa. Prep time 5 Total time 20 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan and take out a baking tray and a deep frying pan or wok. STEP 2 Place the cauliflower in your baking tray, drizzle with some olive oil and season generously with salt and black pepper. Roast for 15-18 mins, shaking halfway through. STEP 3 Next, char the lemon . Place your frying pan over a medium-high heat and slice the lemon in half. Once the pan is hot, place the lemon cut side down into the pan and fry for 2-3 minutes or until golden and charred. Remove and set to the side. Clean the pan if needed (some light browning is OK, but if there’s lots of residual burn it will affect the flavour of the sauce). STEP 4 Remove the stems from the cavolo nero , then chop the leaves into bite sized pieces and add these to the hot pan with a little salt and olive oil. Stir fry on medium heat for 2-3 minutes until it has darkened and has begun to wilt. Drain the butter beans , reserving all of the liquid. Add the butter beans and the lemon & mustard sauce to the pan with the cavolo nero and bring to a gentle simmer and cook for 3–4 minutes, until the beans and greens have softened. Gradually add the reserved bean liquid while the sauce simmers, until you reach a creamy consistency. Squeeze in the juice from one half of the burnt lemon to finish. STEP 5 To serve, ladle the creamy beans into serving bowls, top with the roasted cauliflower and green olive salsa . We recommend squeezing over the other half of lemon but you can omit this if you feel the dish has enough acidity for your preference. Enjoy! title: Creamy Butter Beans With Caramelised Leeks, Charred Courgettes, Drizzled With Olive & Almond Pesto
method:

title: Creamy Coconut Daal With Spice Roasted Cauliflower, Crispy Kale & Coconut Yoghurt
method:
gut health Creamy Coconut Daal With Spice Roasted Cauliflower, Crispy Kale & Coconut Yoghurt Our take on an Indian classic. Protein and fibre-packed red lentils are gently cooked in coconut milk with turmeric, cardamom, ginger, garlic and garam masala, crowned with spice roasted cauliflower bites, crispy kale and cooling coconut yoghurt. Served with fluffy rice. Prep time 5 Total time 25 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 Preheat the oven to 220°C / 200°C fan, then take out a large roasting tray and two saucepans. STEP 2 Cut any larger pieces of cauliflower so they're all roughly 1.5-2cm squared in size. title: Creamy Green Goddess Beans & Courgettes With Organic Cultured Yoghurt, Pistachio Za'atar & Flatbread
method:
new dish Creamy Green Goddess Beans & Courgettes With Organic Cultured Yoghurt, Pistachio Za'atar & Flatbread Tender cannellini beans are simmered in our super green, luxuriously creamy, pesto infused sauce, served with charred courgettes, in this high protein, gut-friendly dish. At home you'll finish it with charred courgettes, creamy organic cultured yoghurt, a crunchy toasted pistachio za'atar topping and garlic flatbread for dipping and dunking. Prep time 5 Total time 15 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Take out a deep frying pan and a second wide frying pan. STEP 2 Place a frying pan over medium heat and add a drizzle of olive oil. While it heats, finely slice the courgette , and add to the pan with a pinch of salt and fry for 8 minutes, or until softened. Drain and rinse the cannellini beans, then add them to the pan along with the green goddess sauce. In a blender, or in a bowl with a hand blender, blend the baby spinach with 50ml of water (double the amount for a 4-person portion) until smooth, then add it to the sauce and beans. We send the spinach separately to ensure the beautiful vibrant green sauce colour, but if don’t have a blender, you can finely chop the spinach instead. Stir everything together and cook for a few minutes until heated through. STEP 3 Add a generous drizzle of olive oil (we recommend a 2 tablespoons, but you could use less) to your other frying pan, along with the pistachio and za'atar . Heat until the nuts are lightly toasted, around 2-3 minutes. Watch carefully to ensure it doesn't burn. Remove all of the oil, nuts and spices from the pan and place into a ramekin or small bowl. Toast the flatbread in the pan for a few minutes to warm through, then cut into quarters. STEP 4 To serve, divide the creamy green goddess beans between serving bowls, then garnish with a few dollops of yoghurt and the pistachio and za'atar drizzle . Serve the flatbread alongside to scoop it up. Enjoy! title: Creamy Kimchi Macaroni With Roasted Broccoli & Garlic Panko Breadcrumbs
method:
high protein Creamy Kimchi Macaroni With Roasted Broccoli & Garlic Panko Breadcrumbs This creamy, umami packed macaroni is an updated, Korean inspired, healthy version of a nostalgic classic. Kimchi not only adds tonnes of flavour but is great for your gut microbiome too. The crispy, garlicky breadcrumbs and the roasted broccoli add even more layers of flavour and texture. It’s comfort food at its best. Prep time 1 Total time 10 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 Boil your kettle and preheat your oven to 200°C / 180°C fan. Take out a large saucepan, a small frying pan and a roasting tray. STEP 2 Cut the broccoli into bite-sized pieces. Lay them on a baking tray in a single layer with a drizzle of olive oil and season with salt and pepper. Roast for 10-15 mins until golden, tossing once. STEP 3 Pour boiling water into your saucepan, add the macaroni pasta, then place over the heat and salt the water well. Boil the pasta for 8 minutes or until al dente. Scoop out a mug of pasta water, then drain and return the pasta to the pan. STEP 4 Place your frying pan over medium heat, and dry fry (without oil) the garlic breadcrumbs mix until lightly toasted. Finely chop the spring onion and keep the white half separate from the green. Chop the vegan kimchi into smaller bite-sized pieces (don’t throw away the juices as they’re packed with flavour and probiotics!). STEP 5 Add the gochujang 'cheese' sa uce , spring onion whites, and kimchi to the pot with the macaroni and stir to heat through. Add a few splashes of pasta water as needed to make the sauce silky. Add the cooked broccoli and stir again until just combined. STEP 6 To serve, ladle the creamy macaroni into bowls and top with the crispy garlic breadcrumbs and the spring onion greens. Enjoy! (NB: If saving some for leftovers, heat it with a splash of vegan milk, reserved pasta water or water, as the pasta will absorb a lot of the sauce as it sits) What’s in your box Gochujang 'Cheese' Sauce Broccoli Vegan Kimchi Garlic Breadcrumbs Macaroni Spring Onions Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Creamy Orzo Al Limone With Crispy Panko Tofu, Roasted Cherry Tomatoes & Tenderstem Broccoli
method:
high protein Creamy Orzo Al Limone With Crispy Panko Tofu, Roasted Cherry Tomatoes & Tenderstem Broccoli Like a risotto, but brighter and lighter, made with orzo in a creamy cashew sauce infused with lemon, mustard and parmesan-style flavours like miso and nutritional yeast. Served with crispy golden panko tofu, sweet roasted cherry tomatoes on the vine and vitamin-rich tenderstem broccoli. It's delicious and nutritious, with almost 40g of protein per serving. Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 200°C / 180°C fan, boil your kettle, and take out a roasting tray, a large saucepan and a frying pan. STEP 2 Fill a saucepan with boiling water and place it over medium heat. Once boiling, add the orzo and cook for 9 minutes, or until al dente. Scoop out a cup of the pasta water for later, then drain the orzo and set it aside. STEP 3 Place the cherry tomatoes and tenderstem broccoli on a baking tray, and drizzle with some good-quality olive oil. Season with salt and pepper, then roast for 10–15 minutes, until the tomatoes begin to burst and the tenderstem broccoli is lightly charred. STEP 4 Take out two large bowls. Place the dry mix into a bowl and measure out 50ml (100ml for a 4-person portion) of plant milk (or water if you don’t have milk). Slowly add the milk or water and stir until you have a thick batter. Season well with salt and pepper and stir thoroughly until combined. Pour the panko into the other bowl and season with salt and pepper. Cut the tofu in half lengthwise, then in half again so you have four thin 'steak' like slices. Pat dry each piece with a sheet of kitchen towel and coat them in the batter, then toss it in the panko. Press gently to ensure the panko evenly covers all sides. STEP 5 Add about 1 cm of neutral oil to a frying pan and place it over medium-low heat. You can just add a drizzle of oil if you prefer but the tofu won't get quite as crispy. Once the oil is hot enough to sizzle when a small amount of panko is added, carefully place the coated tofu in the pan to fry. Turn the tofu as each side becomes crispy, cooking until all sides are golden, around 5 minutes total. Remove from the pan and place on kitchen paper to drain any excess oil. STEP 6 Place the saucepan you used for the orzo over a medium heat, add the creamy lemon sauce and gently warm through for a few minutes. Add the cooked orzo and stir well, then add a little of the reserved pasta water to loosen the sauce to your preferred consistency. Season to taste with salt and pepper. What’s in your box Creamy Lemon Sauce Orzo Cherry Tomatoes on the Vine Organic Tofu Panko Broccoli Dry Mix Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Creamy Panang Curry With Tofu, Red Pepper, Baby Spinach, Sticky Jasmine Rice, Crushed Peanuts & Lime
method:
high protein Creamy Panang Curry With Tofu, Red Pepper, Baby Spinach, Sticky Jasmine Rice, Crushed Peanuts & Lime Panang curry is a type of Thai red curry, featuring aromatic kaffir lime leaves, lemongrass, cumin, coriander and some peanut butter resulting in a thicker, creamier sauce. We're serving it with tender chunks of tofu, vitamin C packed red pepper and iron rich baby spinach, served over sticky jasmine rice and finished with a squeeze of fresh lime and some toasted peanuts for crunch. Prep time 2 Total time 18 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Pre-heat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan, then take out a baking tray, small saucepan and a deep frying pan or wok. STEP 2 Tear the tofu into bitesized chunks, place into your roasting tray, toss through a drizzle of olive oil and season with salt and pepper. Roast for 20 minutes, turning halfway. The tofu should be starting to turn brown and crispy on the outside. STEP 3 Place the jasmine rice into your small saucepan, cover with 1.25–1.5x its volume in water and place over a medium-high heat. Bring to the boil, stir then reduce to low and cover with a lid. Cook until tender and fluffy, around 8-10 minutes. Turn off the heat and leave to steam, covered, until ready to serve. STEP 4 Place your frying pan or wok over a medium heat and dry fry (without oil) the peanuts to toast. Remove and set aside. Lightly crush or chop the peanuts and wipe the pan clean with some kitchen towel. STEP 5 Remove the stem and core from the red pepper and then finely slice. You could also slice some of the red pepper very finely and set aside as a garnish (optional). Once there is around 8 minutes left of cooking time on the tofu, place your frying pan or wok over a medium heat and add a drizzle of oil. Stir fry the red pepper around 3-5 minutes until slightly softened, then add the panang curry sauce and the baby spinach. Stir to combine and cook until the sauce is piping hot and the spinach has wilted, around 3 minutes. Add 50-100ml water (double the amount for a four-person portion) if the sauce reduces too much or starts to stick. Finally, stir through the roasted tofu , squeeze in the juice of half of the lime and stir to combine. Season with salt to taste. STEP 6 To serve up, divide the jasmine rice between serving bowls and spoon over the curry and vegetables. Finish with a scattering of crushed peanuts , lime juice to taste and the red pepper garnish if you made some. Enjoy!
