title: Chaat Masala With Crispy Baby Potatoes, Tamarind Yoghurt, Minted Chilli Dressing & Crispy Sev
method:
23 plants Chaat Masala With Crispy Baby Potatoes, Tamarind Yoghurt, Minted Chilli Dressing & Crispy Sev Our spin on a popular Indian street food recipe, with layers upon layers of flavour. Crispy baby potatoes and plump chickpeas are fried in a fruity, spicy chaat masala spice paste, then smothered in zingy chilli mint chutney and tangy tamarind yoghurt. Finished with a scattering of crispy chickpea sev. Prep time 3 Total time 20 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 200°C / 180°C fan. Take out two baking trays and a small frying pan. STEP 2 Slice the baby potatoes into thin slices, about 3mm thick. Toss them in olive oil, salt, and pepper, then spread evenly on one of the baking trays. Roast in the oven for 15 minutes, then stir in the chaat masala paste along with the drained chickpeas . Return to the oven for a further 5 minutes, or until the paste caramelises at the edges of the potatoes. STEP 3 Peel and finely slice the red onion . Onion sizes vary, but aim for around half a small onion per person. Dice the tomato and cucumber , then add them to a bowl along with half of the mint chutney . Season to taste and give everything a good mix. STEP 4 Place your small frying pan over medium heat and dry-toast (without oil) the sev for 1–2 minutes, until crispy. STEP 5 Divide the chickpea and potato chaat between serving plates, top with the tomato, cucumber salad then dress with the remaining mint chutney , tamarind yoghurt , red onion , and a scattering of sev . Enjoy! title: White Bean Kale Caesar Salad With Ciabatta Croutons, Caper Caesar Dressing & Happy Hormone Seed Sprinkle
method:
high protein White Bean Kale Caesar Salad With Ciabatta Croutons, Caper Caesar Dressing & Happy Hormone Seed Sprinkle Our take one of the most delicious salads ever invented, fully loaded with cavolo nero, baby gem, white beans, golden ciabatta croutons, almond parmesan and a caper-spiked cashew caesar dressing. Full of flavour, packed with nutrition and perfect for al fresco dining. Make this gluten-free by omitting the croutons. Prep time 5 Total time 10 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 200°C / 180°C fan and take out a roasting tray, a small frying pan, and a large mixing bowl. STEP 2 Roughly cut or tear the ciabatta into chunky croutons, around 2cm cubes. Place them on your baking tray, toss with a drizzle of olive oil, and season with salt and pepper. You can also add a grated garlic clove for extra flavour (optional). Roast until golden, about 10 minutes. STEP 3 Remove any tough stems from the cavolo nero , then shred the leaves and place them in a mixing bowl. Add a pinch of salt and a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil, then massage it firmly with your hands for 2–3 minutes, or until softened and slightly wilted. Alternatively, if you prefer not to eat it raw, you can steam or boil it for 5–10 minutes. STEP 4 Cut off the base of the baby gem lettuce , then slice into bite-sized pieces. Open and drain the cannellini beans . STEP 5 Place your small frying pan over medium heat and toast the happy hormone seed sprinkle until fragrant. STEP 6 Just before serving, add the cavolo nero , baby gem , ciabatta croutons , cannellini beans , and around two-thirds of the caesar dressing to a mixing bowl. Toss well to coat everything evenly. Divide the salad between serving bowls, drizzle over the remaining dressing, and sprinkle over the toasted happy hormone seed sprinkle . Enjoy! title: Kimchi Pancakes With Sticky Soy Portobellos, Gochujang Mayo & Pickled Cucumbers
method:
gut health Kimchi Pancakes With Sticky Soy Portobellos, Gochujang Mayo & Pickled Cucumbers Prep time 5 Total time 15 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 Take out two large frying pans (ideally one non-stick!). Cut the pak choi into 1-1.5cm pieces from the tip to the base, discarding the base. Slice the spring onions , keeping the greens and whites separate. Slice the portobello mushrooms 0.5cm thick. STEP 2 Heat a large pan with a good drizzle of olive oil. Add the portobello mushrooms and spring onion (white bits) along with plenty of pepper and cook for about 5 minutes on a medium heat. Don't stir them too often as it will stop them caramelising. STEP 3 Once the portobello mushrooms have started to soften, add the pak choi and cook for another 3-4 minutes until the leaves have softened and the stems still have a little bite to them. Then add the sticky mushroom sauce to the pan and stir well to combine. STEP 4 To make the kimchi pancakes , pour the dry mix into a large bowl and slowly add 230ml of water while whisking vigorously to avoid any lumps. Roughly chop the kimchi and add to the bowl too. STEP 5 Heat about half a tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large non-stick frying pan. Once hot, pour half of the pancake batter , then tilt the pan in a circular motion so the batter spreads evenly around the pan (use the back of a spoon to spread it out to the edges if needed). Cook for 2-3 minutes on each side. You can slide the pancake off onto a plate or chopping board and then turn the pan upside down onto the plate to help you flip it. Repeat with the second pancake. STEP 6 To serve, layer the portobello mushro oms and pak choi over one half of the pancake , and fold it over. Top with the gochujang mayo and remaining spring onion (green bits) and sesame seeds and serve with a side of pickled cucumber . Enjoy! title: Kimchi Stir Fried Noodles With Crispy Tempeh Mince, Cabbage, Carrot, Spring Onions & Sesame Gochugaru Crunch
method:
new dish Kimchi Stir Fried Noodles With Crispy Tempeh Mince, Cabbage, Carrot, Spring Onions & Sesame Gochugaru Crunch Tender udon noodles coated in a spicy, umami-rich sauce, with fibre-rich cabbage and naturally sweet, beta-carotene-rich carrots, crispy fried tempeh mince and tangy kimchi. All finished with fresh spring onions and a crunchy sesame gochugaru topping. They're high in protein and great for your gut thanks to fermented foods like tempeh, kimchi and gochujang. Prep time 3 Total time 15 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Boil your kettle, then take out a saucepan and a large frying pan or wok. Pour the boiling water from the kettle into the saucepan and place it over medium-high heat. STEP 2 Add the udon noodles to the saucepan of boiling water and cook until al dente, about 4–5 minutes. Drain the noodles, rinse them under cold water, and set them aside in a colander to drain. STEP 3 Crumble the tempeh into small, mince-like pieces. Heat the frying pan or wok over medium-high heat, add a generous drizzle of oil, and fry the tempeh until crispy, about 6–8 minutes. Season with a dash of soy sauce, if you have some (if not, use salt), and pepper. Transfer the tempeh to a bowl, cover to keep warm, and set aside. STEP 4 Peel the carrot into ribbons, cut the white cabbage into bite-sized pieces, and slice the spring onions , separating the white and green parts. Wipe the frying pan or wok clean, then return it to a high heat. Add a drizzle of olive oil, then add the carrot ribbons, white cabbage, and the white parts of the spring onions. Cook for a couple of minutes to soften. Turn the heat down to medium, pour in the kimchi noodle stir fry sauce , and add the drained udon noodles . Toss everything together until the noodles and vegetables are evenly coated in the sauce. Add a splash of water if needed to prevent burning or sticking. STEP 5 To serve, divide the noodles between plates. Top with the crispy tempeh , some kimchi and the spring onion greens. Finish with a sprinkle of the sesa me gochugaru crunch . Enjoy! title: Lahmacun Flatbread With Meaty Spiced Tofu Crumble, Tomato, Cucumber, Sumac Pickled Onion & Garlic Yoghurt
method:
Lahmacun Flatbread With Meaty Spiced Tofu Crumble, Tomato, Cucumber, Sumac Pickled Onion & Garlic Yoghurt Our plant-based spin on the Middle Eastern flatbread, traditionally topped with minced meat. We've swapped in crumbled tofu in a spiced tomato and red pepper marinade, infused with spices and pomegranate molasses for richness, then fried until golden and crispy. Topped with a fresh, crunchy salad of tomato, parsley, cucumber, sumac pickled cabbage and onion, all finished with garlic lemon yoghurt. Wrap it up or cut it into pizza slices! Prep time 3 Total time 20 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan and take out two mixing bowls and a baking tray. STEP 2 Remove the tofu from the packaging and squeeze out as much water as possible. Crumble it into one of the mixing bowls with your hands, add ⅔ of the lahmacun sauce and mix until well combined. Season with salt, pepper and a drizzle of olive oil. STEP 3 Lay the flatbreads on the baking tray and divide the lahmacun tofu mixture evenly between them, spreading it across the top in a thin layer. Bake for 15–20 minutes, until the flatbread edges are golden and the tofu layer is crispy on top. STEP 4 Meanwhile, dice the cucumber and tomato . Pick and roughly chop the parsley leaves . Reserve half of the parsley for garnish, then combine the remaining parsley with the cucumber and tomato in your second mixing bowl. Season with salt, pepper and a drizzle of olive oil, then set aside to marinate. STEP 5 To serve, drizzle the remaining lahmacun sauce over the lahmacun flatbreads and top with the tomato and cucumber salad , pickled red onion and cabbage , a drizzle of garlic yoghurt and a scattering of fresh parsley. We like cutting it up into slices like a pizza, but you could eat it with a knife and fork or wrap it up. Enjoy! title: Gut-Friendly Lemon & Chickpea Broth With Crispy Parsnip & Sweet Potato, Cavolo Nero & Salsa Verde
method:
gut health Gut-Friendly Lemon & Chickpea Broth With Crispy Parsnip & Sweet Potato, Cavolo Nero & Salsa Verde This wholesome and vibrant stew contains everything you want in a bowl at this time of year. Featuring a rich and flavoursome preserved lemon and white wine broth, filled with a medley of fresh vegetables and plump chickpeas, all finished with a generous drizzle of zingy salsa verde. Full of flavour and packed with protein, fibre and vitamins to help you look and feel your best. Prep time 4 Total time 20 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 200°C / 220°C fan, then take out a large roasting tray, a steamer and a saucepan. STEP 2 Cut the top and tail off the parsnip and sweet potato , then dice into small 1cm cubes. Place into your roasting tray, toss in a drizzle of olive oil and season well with salt and pepper. Roast for 15-20 minutes or until tender and crispy. STEP 3 Remove any hardy stems from the cavolo nero , then roughly chop the leaves into bite-sized pieces. Steam for for 5-10 minutes or until tender. If you don't have a steamer you can boil for the same time. STEP 4 Drain the chickpeas , then empty into your large saucepan along with the lemon broth and 200ml of water. Place over a medium high heat, bring to the boil, then reduce the heat and continue to cook for 2-3 minutes or until the stew is piping hot and beans are cooked through. You can continue to reduce the broth for longer if you prefer a stronger flavour. STEP 5 To serve up, divide the stew between serving bowls, top with the cavolo nero and roasted sweet potato and parsnips . Drizzle generously with the tangy caper salsa verde , and enjoy! title: Lemon & Ginger Detox Ramen With Smoked Tofu, Udon Noodles, Cavolo Nero, Carrot & Superseed Chilli Crunch
method:
Lemon & Ginger Detox Ramen With Smoked Tofu, Udon Noodles, Cavolo Nero, Carrot & Superseed Chilli CrunchA delicious flavoursome ramen broth, made by slowly simmering medicinal ingredients like shiitake, garlic, ginger, lemon and soy . Filled with meaty, protein-rich smoked tofu, tender chewy udon noodles, colourful vegetables and a final drizzle of punchy, umami-rich superseed chilli crunch drizzle.Prep time5Total time15Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructionsBefore you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below).Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grainsbefore cooking or serving.STEP 1Boil your kettle and take out a large saucepan and a frying pan.STEP 2Remove the tough stems from thecavolo neroand tear the leaves into bite-sized pieces.Trim and peel thecarrot, then cut it into matchsticks (julienne). Alternatively, use a vegetable peeler to create thin ribbons.Place your saucepan over a medium heat and fill it with the boiling water from the kettle. Add a few pinches of salt. Cook the cavolo nero for 2–3 minutes until bright green and just wilted, while still retaining a little bite. Using a slotted spoon, transfer it to a bowl and season lightly with salt or a dash of soy sauce.You can cook the carrot in the same water for 1–2 minutes until just tender, or leave it raw if you prefer a little extra crunch and freshness in your ramen. Season lightly with salt or a dash of soy sauce and set aside.STEP 3Empty the saucepan and refill it with fresh boiling water. Add theudon noodlesand cook for 5–6 minutes, or until tender but still al dente.Drain and rinse the noodles under cold water to remove excess starch and prevent them from becoming sticky. Leave them in a colander to drain.STEP 4Place the saucepan back over a low heat and add theshiitake & ginger lemon brothalong with 150–200ml of water (double the quantity for 4 people). Bring to a gentle simmer.STEP 5In the meantime, place your frying pan over a medium-high heat.Cut thelemonin half and slice two thin rounds for garnish. Set these aside. Place the remaining lemon halves, cut-side down, into the hot pan and cook until lightly charred, about 2–3 minutes.Squeeze the juice from the charred lemon into the simmering broth, tasting as you go to adjust the amount to your preference.STEP 6Wipe the frying pan clean and return it to a medium-high heat with a drizzle of oil.Slice thesmoked tofuin half horizontally, then add it to the pan. Cook for 3–5 minutes on each side until lightly golden. You could add a dash of soy sauce if you have some at the end of cooking to season and caramelise the tofu, otherwise just season with salt and pepper. Remove from the heat and slice into approximately 1.5cm-thick pieces (refer to the image above).STEP 7Just before serving, add the drainedudon noodlesto the simmering broth and warm through for 1 minute.Divide the noodles and broth between serving bowls. Top with thecavolo nero,carrotandsmoked tofu. Drizzle over thesuperspeed chilli crunchand finish with a slice oflemon. Enjoy!What’s in your boxShiitake & Ginger Lemon BrothSuperseed Chilli CrunchLemonSmoked TofuCavolo NeroCarrotUdon NoodlesNutritional infoAll nutritional values are based on a per person scale.Calories878kcalTotal fat36.1gSaturated fat4.6gSalt3.6gTotal carbohydrate104.8gTotal sugar14.3gFibre11gProtein36.4gIngredients / AllergensGarlic, ginger, shiitake mushroom, raw organic agave nectar, water, gluten free low sodiumsoysauce, sea salt, lemon, carrot, udon noodles (wheat flour (gluten), sea salt), rapeseed oil,sesameoil, rice vinegar, smoked paprika, gochugaru,sesameseeds, sunflower seeds, pumpkin seeds, linseed,peanuts, organic smoked tofu (water,soybeans,nigari, sea salt)For allergens, please see ingredients inboldPlease be advised that we handle the following allergens in our kitchen: CELERY, SOYA, GLUTEN, NUTS, PEANUTS, MUSTARD, SESAME SEEDS, SULPHITES

title: Lion's Mane Steak With Chimichurri, Crispy Baby Potatoes, Watercress & Béarnaise sauce
method:
Lion's Mane Steak With Chimichurri, Crispy Baby Potatoes, Watercress & Béarnaise sauce If you've never tried Lions Mane Steak, prepare to be blown away. It has a significantly meatier texture and flavour profile than other mushroom varieties which works perfectly as a steak. We're serving it with crispy potatoes, peppery British watercress and a classic chimichurri dressing and béarnaise sauce. This meal includes premium ingredients and comes with a one-off additional charge. For existing subscribers, this will be added on top of your usual subscription rate for one week only. For new customers, the surcharge will be included in your first order total only. Prep time 2 Total time 25 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan. Take out a large cast iron skillet or frying pan and two roasting trays. You will also need a tea towel and a grill press, heavy saucepan/frying pan, or a flat metal lid to press down the lion's mane mushroom steaks while searing. STEP 2 Cut the baby potatoes into roughly 1.5-2cm cubed pieces (cutting into halves, thirds or quarters works well, depending on potato size). Place them on one of the roasting trays, drizzle with olive oil, and season well with salt and pepper. Roast on the lower shelf of the oven for 20–25 minutes, or until golden and crispy. STEP 3 Place your skillet or frying pan over medium-high heat and add a generous glug of olive oil, or enough to lightly coat the base of the pan. Once the skillet is hot and nearly smoking, place the whole lion's mane mushrooms stem-side down in the pan. As the grill press will become very hot during cooking, fold your tea towel into a thick, heat-resistant pad to grasp the handle of your grill press, then press down on the mushroom and cook for 4–6 minutes, until the juices have evaporated, the bottom begins to form a golden crust and the mushroom has started to flatten. You don't need to continually press for the entire cooking time - pressing periodically as the mushroom cooks works well. Season generously with salt and freshly ground black pepper, then flip the mushrooms and press down again firmly on the other side. Continue cooking until the mushrooms have released their juices and the liquid has evaporated completely, about 6-8 minutes. Both sides should develop a deep golden, caramelised crust. Depending on the size and moisture content of the mushrooms, the searing time may vary. Remove from the heat and transfer to the other roasting tray. *We recommend leaving the lion's mane mushrooms whole, as this helps create a juicy, meaty, steak-like texture. STEP 4 Place the roasting tray with searred lion's mane mushrooms in the top shelf of the oven, and bake for 10-15 minutes. If the baby potatoes are ready before the mushrooms, cover them with a sheet of aluminium foil to prevent further browning, or remove them from the oven and cover to keep warm. STEP 5 You can serve the lion's mane 'steaks' whole on each plate, or slice them on a chopping board, then serve (as shown in the image). If you like, you can toss the watercress in a little chimichurri dressing , before drizzling the remainder over the steaks. Serve up the roasted baby potatoes , a handful of watercress and a dollop of béarnaise sauce on the side. Enjoy! title: Mac & Greens With Tenderstem Broccoli, Crispy Kale & Mezzi Rigatoni In A Creamy Cashew Sauce
method:
family favourites Mac & Greens With Tenderstem Broccoli, Crispy Kale & Mezzi Rigatoni In A Creamy Cashew Sauce Our plant-based spin on one of the world's greatest comfort foods is exactly what you want to eat at this time of year. It's so delicious and so nutritious at the same time. Made with a creamy, 'cheesy' cashew based sauce, tossed through mezzi rigatoni with lots of vibrant, healthy green vegetables including tenderstem broccoli, garden peas and crispy kale. It's packed with 32g of protein, 22g of fibre and lots of essential vitamins and minerals. Prep time 2 Total time 15 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 Boil your kettle, preheat your oven to 180°C / 160°C fan and take out a large saucepan and a large baking tray. STEP 2 Remove any hardy stems from the green kale and then roughly chop the leaves. Cut the broccoli into bite-sized pieces, place into your roasting tray, toss in a little olive oil and season well with salt and pepper. Roast for 10 minutes, then add the kale with a little olive oil and seasoning tossed through and roast everything for a further 5 minutes. STEP 3 Meanwhile pour boiling water from your kettle into your other saucepan and place over a high heat. Once boiling add the mezzi rigatoni and cook until al dente, around 12 minutes. Drain the pasta and set aside to keep warm. STEP 4 Place the same saucepan you used for your pasta back over a low-medium heat and pour in the cashew 'cheese' sauce . Heat through for 1-2 minutes, then remove from the heat and stir through the pasta and half of the broccoli and kale . Divide the pasta between serving bowls and garnish with the remaining broccoli and kale . Season to taste with salt and pepper, and enjoy! What’s in your box Cashew 'Cheese' Sauce with Garden Peas Broccoli Green Kale Mezzi Rigatoni Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Mango Crispy Rice Salad With Smacked Cucumber, Avocado, Black Beans, Toasted Cashews, Tahini & Chilli Crunch Dressing
method:
Mango Crispy Rice Salad With Smacked Cucumber, Avocado, Black Beans, Toasted Cashews, Tahini & Chilli Crunch Dressing This salad is delicious, satiating, full of protein and fibre, as well as crunchy fresh textures and flavours. Featuring rice roasted in a little chilli dressing until crispy. Tossed with juicy mango, smacked cucumber, black beans for protein, avocado and toasted cashews for healthy fats, and finished with coriander, chilli dressing and tahini sauce for punchy flavours. Prep time 5 Total time 25 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan. Boil your kettle, then take out a saucepan, a roasting tray, and a large mixing bowl. Line the roasting tray with a sheet of parchment paper. STEP 2 Rinse the sushi rice under cold water until the water runs mostly clear, then drain well. Add it to a saucepan with 1.25–1.5 times its volume of cold water and, if you have time, leave it to soak for 10–15 minutes. Bring to the boil over a medium heat, then cover, reduce the heat to low, and cook for 10 minutes without lifting the lid. Once cooked, remove from the heat and fluff the rice with a fork to release the steam. Stir through half of the chilli oil until evenly coated. Spread the rice in a thin, even layer over the lined roasting tray. For extra crispiness, you can lightly brush or spray the rice with a little oil if you have some to hand. Roast for 15–20 minutes, or until the rice is crisp and golden. Add the cashews to the tray for the final 5 minutes of cooking to toast. STEP 3 Trim the ends off the baby cucumber and place it on a chopping board. Gently smash it along its length using a rolling pin or the flat side of a heavy pestle until it splits, then chop diagonally into bite-sized pieces. Transfer to the large mixing bowl and toss with the remaining chilli oil . STEP 4 Meanwhile, peel, stone and dice the avocado and mango into bite-sized pieces. Drain and rinse the black beans . Pick the coriander leaves from the stalks. Cut the lime into wedges and finely slice the red chilli for garnish. STEP 5 When the crispy rice is ready, add it to the bowl with the smashed cucumber , along with the avocado , mango , black beans and coriander leaves . Toss everything together until well combined. STEP 6 Divide the crispy rice salad between serving bowls. Top with the toasted cashews , drizzle over the tahini dressing , and finish with a squeeze of fresh lime juice and the sliced red chilli . Enjoy! title: Mango Soba Noodle Salad With Avocado, Edamame, Cashews, Pickled Radish, Toasted Seeds & Crispy Onions
method:
new dish Mango Soba Noodle Salad With Avocado, Edamame, Cashews, Pickled Radish, Toasted Seeds & Crispy Onions Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Boil your kettle, then take out a frying pan and a saucepan. Pour the boiling water from the kettle into the saucepan and place it over a medium heat. Once the water is bubbling, boil the edamame for 2-3 minutes then remove with a slotted spoon (so you can use the same water for the noodles), drain and rinse under cold water. Add the soba noodles and cook for 5–6 minutes, or until tender but still al dente. Once cooked, drain the noodles, rinse them under cold water, and set them aside in a colander to drain thoroughly. STEP 2 Remove the stem from the red chilli . Finely mince half of it and mix it with the mango soba noodle dressing in a small bowl. Thinly slice the rest of the chilli for garnish. Red chillies can vary in spiciness depending on the season, so always taste a little first to check the spiciness and decide how much you'd like to use. Add the minced chilli gradually to the dressing, tasting as you go, to make sure the heat level is just right for you! STEP 3 Place your frying pan over a medium heat and dry fry (without oil) the crispy onions, nuts & seeds for 2-3 minutes, tossing occasionally until lightly toasted. STEP 4 Peel and destone the avocado and mango . Cut the avocado into 2 cm chunks and slice the mango into thin strips. Roughly chop the coriander . STEP 5 To serve, divide the soba noodles between serving bowls, then top with the avocado , mango , pickled radish , and edamame . Drizzle over the mango soba noodle dressing and give them a light toss. Sprinkle with the toasted crispy onions, nuts and seeds , then finish with the chopped coriander and red chilli slices . Enjoy! title: Mapo Tofu Udon Noodles In Creamy Gochujang Sauce With Fresh Cucumber Salad & Toasted Sesame Seeds
method:
Mapo Tofu Udon Noodles In Creamy Gochujang Sauce With Fresh Cucumber Salad & Toasted Sesame Seeds Our spin on the viral mapo udon noodles packs almost 40g protein per serving. Featuring creamy gochujang noodles, topped with crispy ground tofu tossed in a mouth-tingling sichuan pepper and fermented black bean sauce. All finished with a fresh cucumber salad, sesame seeds and spring onions. Prep time 4 Total time 15 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Boil your kettle, then take out a saucepan and a large frying pan. STEP 2 Slice the cucumber into matchsticks, add a pinch of salt and a squeeze of half a lime , and set aside to marinate. Cut the remaining lime into wedges for garnish. Separate the white and green parts of the spring onions . Roughly chop the white parts and thinly slice the green parts. STEP 3 Place your frying pan over medium-high heat and add a drizzle of oil (sesame oil is recommended if you have some). Crumble the tofu into roughly minced pieces and add to the pan along with the white parts of the spring onion . Stir-fry for 4–5 minutes until the tofu starts to turn golden. Stir in the sichuan black bean tofu sauce and cook for a further 2–3 minutes until fragrant and the tofu begins to crisp at the edges. STEP 4 Meanwhile, add boiling water from the kettle to your saucepan and place it over medium-high heat. Once the water is bubbling, add the udon noodles and cook for 4–5 minutes, or until tender but still al dente. Once cooked, drain and rinse the noodles under cold water. This helps remove excess starch and prevents them from becoming soggy. Set aside in a colander to drain. STEP 5 Boil your kettle again, then place the colander with the rinsed udon noodles over the sink and pour boiling water over them to warm through. Drain well, then return them to the saucepan. Place over medium heat, add the creamy gochujang sauce , and mix well while gently warming for 1–2 minutes. STEP 6 To serve, divide the creamy gochujang udon noodles between bowls and top with the sichuan mapo tofu mince . Add the marinated cucumber on the side and garnish with the green parts of the spring onion and a wedge of lime . Sprinkle over the sesame garnish and sliced chilli or chilli flakes if you would like extra heat (optional). Enjoy! title: Massaman Tofu & Potato Curry With Toasted Flaked Almonds, Organic Cultured Yoghurt, Blistered Green Beans & Garlic Naan
method:
Massaman Tofu & Potato Curry With Toasted Flaked Almonds, Organic Cultured Yoghurt, Blistered Green Beans & Garlic Naan A rich and creamy peanut and lemongrass infused Thai curry, mildly spiced with ginger, cardamom and cloves, served with roasted tofu, potato and blistered green beans. Finished with organic cultured yoghurt, toasted flaked almonds and served with pillowy soft garlic naan. Prep time 3 Total time 20 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan. Take out a roasting tray, a griddle pan or frying pan, and a saucepan. STEP 2 Tear the tofu into bite-sized chunks with your hands, aiming for pieces about 1.5–2 cm in size. Cut the baby potatoes into similar-sized pieces and add them to the roasting tray. Drizzle over some oil and season with salt and pepper. Place in the oven and roast for 18–20 minutes, or until golden brown and crispy at the edges. STEP 3 Place a griddle pan or frying pan over medium heat and dry roast (without oil) the flaked almonds until golden. Remove from the pan and set aside. STEP 4 Return the pan to medium-high heat and add a drizzle of olive oil. While it heats up, trim the green beans and cut them into bite-sized pieces (or leave them whole if you prefer), then add them to the pan. Fry until they begin to char and blister, around 6–8 minutes. Season to taste with salt and pepper. STEP 5 When the tofu and potatoes have 5 minutes to go, place the garlic & herb flatbreads in the oven to warm for 2–3 minutes. At the same time, place your saucepan over medium heat, add the massaman curry sauce, and warm until piping hot, about 2–3 minutes. Once the potatoes and tofu are ready, remove them from the oven, add them to the warmed sauce, and stir to combine. STEP 6 To serve, divide the tofu and potato massaman curry between serving bowls. Serve with the flatbreads and blistered green beans on the side. Finish with a dollop of organic soy yoghurt and a scattering of toasted flaked almonds . Enjoy! title: Middle Eastern Aubergine Flatbreads With 'Labneh', Pomegranate, Crispy Chickpeas, Garlic Tahini & Fresh Mint
method:
new dish Middle Eastern Aubergine Flatbreads With 'Labneh', Pomegranate, Crispy Chickpeas, Garlic Tahini & Fresh Mint Aubergine roasted in a Middle Eastern spice mix until crispy on the outside and tender in the centre, served over creamy cashew labneh with sweet and tangy pomegranate seeds, crispy chickpeas for protein, silky garlic tahini sauce and fresh mint. All wrapped up in our signature freshly baked, pillowy soft flatbreads. It's all made in one tray so it's quick, easy, nutritious and delicious. Prep time 3 Total time 25 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan, take out two baking trays and a frying pan. STEP 2 Slice the aubergine into 1cm-thick rounds and place them on one of your baking trays. Add a generous drizzle of extra virgin olive oil, sprinkle with the aubergine spice mix , and season with salt to taste. Toss to coat each piece evenly, then spread them out in a single layer. Drain, rinse, and pat dry the chickpeas , then add them to the other baking tray. Drizzle with extra virgin olive oil, season with salt and freshly ground black pepper, toss to coat, and spread them out in a single layer as well. Place both trays in the oven and roast until the aubergine is soft and beginning to char, and the chickpeas are golden and crispy, about 20-25 minutes. STEP 3 Once everything is ready, warm the flatbre ads by dry frying (without oil) in your frying pan on both sides for 30-60 seconds or until lightly toasted. You can alternatively bake or grill them in the oven until warmed through. STEP 4 To serve, place a warmed flatbread on each plate and spread the labneh in the centre. Top with the roasted aubergine and crispy chickpeas , then drizzle over the garlic tahini sauce . Finish with a garnish of pomegranate seeds and picked mint leaves. Enjoy! title: Kimchi Miso Nourish Bowl With Farro, Charred Purple Sprouting Broccoli & Pak Choi
method:
gut health Kimchi Miso Nourish Bowl With Farro, Charred Purple Sprouting Broccoli & Pak Choi A delicious and aromatic fried rice dish, with a few healthy swaps including farro grains, which are high in gut-loving fibre! Protein-packed purple sprouting broccoli, pak choi and peas are cooked until tender, then tossed through nutty brown rice grains, along with tangy chopped kimchi and an umami-rich miso dressing. All freshened up with sliced spring onion and red chilli. Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Take out a saucepan and a large wok or frying pan. STEP 2 Bring a pan of lightly salted water to the boil in your saucepan, add the farro grains and cook until tender, around 15-20 minutes. Drain and set aside. STEP 3 Slice the base off the pak choi and pull apart the leaves with your hands. Cut the broccoli into bite-sized pieces. Trim the roots from the spring onions, then cut them in two where the pale green and white parts meet. Cut the whites into 4cm logs. Finely slice the greens. Keep the greens and whites separate. Dice the kimchi and finely slice the red chilli . STEP 4 Once the farro has been cooking for 10 minutes, place your large wok or frying pan over a medium-high heat and add a drizzle of oil. Once hot, add the thicker spring onion logs, broccoli and pak choi and cook for around 5 minutes or until lightly charred and tender. STEP 5 Stir through the cooked farro , kimchi , miso dressing and peas and cook until the peas are cooked through, around 1-2 minutes. STEP 6 Divide the the farro and vegetables between serving bowls, then scatter over the remaining half of finely sliced spring onions and the sliced red chilli . Enjoy!. What’s in your box Miso Dressing Vegan Kimchi Farro Pak Choi Broccoli (Purple Sprouting / Tenderstem) Garden Peas Red Chilli Spring Onions Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Miso Mushroom Orzotto With Creamy Cashew 'Labneh' & Chilli Pine Nut Crumb
method:
high protein Miso Mushroom Orzotto With Creamy Cashew 'Labneh' & Chilli Pine Nut Crumb This creamy, umami packed dish has four elements that come together to make every bite perfectly balanced: a cashew based creamy miso sauce that’s stirred through the orzo, the caramelised mushrooms, a moorish pine nut crumb with a tiny hint of chilli and the light but decadent vegan stracciatella style cheese to top it all off. It’s comforting but light and a really delicious pasta. Prep time 3 Total time 15 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving.. STEP 1 Boil your kettle, then take out two large saucepans. STEP 2 Place one of your saucepans over medium-high heat, pour in boiling water from your kettle, and add salt as you would for cooking pasta. Once the water is bubbling, add the orzo and cook for 9 minutes or until al dente. Before draining, reserve a mugful of pasta water, then drain the orzo. STEP 3 Meanwhile, finely chop the chives and set them aside. Then, thinly slice the chestnut mushrooms . Place another saucepan over medium-high heat and add a generous drizzle of olive oil. Add the sliced mushrooms and sauté for 6–8 minutes until nicely caramelised and browned at the edges. Once the mushrooms are ready, pour in the miso cream and stir occasionally to prevent sticking while warming it to a gentle simmer. STEP 4 Stir the cooked orzo into the warmed miso cream along with the baby spinach and cook until wilted, around 2 minutes. Season to taste with salt and add a splash of reserved pasta water if the sauce needs loosening any more. STEP 5 To serve, divide the orzotto between two plates and top with the creamy cashew labneh , plenty of cracked black pepper, the pine nut crumb , chopped chives and a final drizzle of olive oil if you like. Enjoy! What’s in your box Miso Cream Orzo Chestnut Mushrooms Baby Spinach Creamy Cashew Labneh Chives Pine Nut Crumb Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Cheesy Pumpkin & Cauliflower Gratin With Tender Butter Beans, Cavolo Nero & Crispy Sage
method:
new dish Cheesy Pumpkin & Cauliflower Gratin With Tender Butter Beans, Cavolo Nero & Crispy Sage A delicious and seriously comforting one pot dish, featuring all the flavours you crave in autumn and winter. Our rich, roasted pumpkin sauce is infused with miso and nutritional yeast for 'cheesy' umami depth, stirred through cauliflower florets, cavolo nero and butter beans, all roasted until bubbling and golden, then finished with crispy sag Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan. Take out a frying pan and a shallow casserole or baking dish (~25-30cm diameter / 4-5cm depth). NB: If you don't have a shallow baking dish, we recommend roasting the cauliflower separately in a baking tray, heating the sauce through in a pot on the hob and then combining. STEP 2 Cut the cauliflower into bite-sized pieces and season with a glug of olive oil, salt, and freshly ground black pepper. STEP 3 Drain the butter beans very thoroughly and add them to your ovenproof dish along with the cauliflower florets and the cheesy pumpkin sauce . Stir well so everything is mixed together. Place the dish in the oven to bake for 25-35 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and the top layer of sauce and cauliflower is browning. STEP 4 When the gratin is 10 minutes from being ready, remove the tough stems from the cavolo nero and slice the leaves into bite-sized pieces. Place your frying pan over medium heat, add a drizzle of olive oil, and sauté the kale for 5-6 minutes or until tender and with slightly crispy edges. We recommend adding a splash of water to help the kale steam a little in the pan. Season with salt and pepper to taste, then remove from the pan to keep warm STEP 6 Wipe the frying pan you used for the kale with a sheet of kitchen towel and place it back over medium heat. Pick the sage leaves off their stems and add them to the hot oil for around 20-30 seconds, until crispy and aromatic. The leaves will turn slightly translucent when ready. STEP 6 To serve, divide the gratin between serving bowls and top with the crispy sage leaves and a drizzle of the aromatic oil. Serve alongside the sautéed kale and enjoy! What’s in your box Cheesy Pumpkin Sauce Cauliflower Florets Butterbeans Cavolo Nero Sage Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Mixed Vegetable Paella With Courgette, Red Pepper, Fennel, Garden Peas, Fresh Parsley & Lemon
method:
gut health Mixed Vegetable Paella With Courgette, Red Pepper, Fennel, Garden Peas, Fresh Parsley & Lemon Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 Take out a large frying pan and a wide pan (approx 20-30cm) with a lid to cook your paella in. You can cover with foil if you don't have a pan with a lid, but ensure you are using a wide deep pan so the paella cooks evenly. Slice the courgette into 1cm rounds and cut the fennel into wedges. STEP 2 Place your wide deep pan over a high heat. Add a drizzle of olive oil to your pan and toast the paella rice until translucent, around 1 minute. Add the paella sauce and 300ml of water to the pan and combine well with the toasted paella rice. Bring to the boil, then reduce the heat to medium and simmer on a medium heat without stirring for 15 minutes, until most of the liquid has been absorbed by the rice. If the rice looks dry when 15 minutes is up, top it up with a bit more stock / water so that there is a thin layer of moisture on top of the rice. STEP 3 While the paella is simmering, char the courgette slices and fennel wedges in a hot and lightly oiled frying pan. Add the garden peas for the final 2-3 minutes of cooking time to warm through. Season and set aside. STEP 4 Once the paella has been simmering for 15 minutes, reduce the heat to the lowest setting and cover the paella dish with a tight fitting lid or a piece of kitchen foil and allow the rice to finish cooking in its steam, around 5 minutes. STEP 5 Top the paella with your charred courgette and fennel , garden peas , a generous sprinkle of chopped parsley and a squeeze of fresh lemon . Season with sea salt and freshly cracked black pepper and enjoy! What’s in your box Paella Sauce Paella Rice Garden Peas Parsley Fennel Courgette Lemon Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Mushroom Birria Tacos With Melted House Queso, Onion Salsa & Rich Dipping Consommé
method:
Mushroom Birria Tacos With Melted House Queso, Onion Salsa & Rich Dipping Consommé Meaty oyster mushrooms, shredded and marinated, then stuffed into warm freshly made corn tortillas with house 'queso', fried and served with a fresh onion, coriander and lime salsa, and a side of deliciously rich, slow-cooked consommé infused with two types of Mexican chillies. Prep time 3 Total time 20 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Preheat the oven to 220°C/200°C fan. Take out a large roasting tray, a frying pan, and a small saucepan. STEP 2 Tear the oyster mushrooms into bite-sized pieces. Peel and halve the brown onion , then thinly slice one half and finely dice the other half. Set aside the diced onion for STEP 4. Place the frying pan over a medium-high heat and add a drizzle of oil. Once hot, add the oyster mushrooms and sliced onion in a single layer. Sauté for 6–8 minutes, until softened and beginning to caramelise. Add half of the consomme and stir well to combine. Cook for a further minute, then remove from the heat. STEP 3 Place the remaining consomme in the saucepan with 100ml water (double for 4 people) and stir to combine. Dip the corn tortillas into the consomme mixture one at a time, then lay them on the roasting tray in a single layer. Divide the sautéed oyster mushrooms and onion between the tortillas, then drizzle over the house queso . Roast in the oven for 6–8 minutes, until the tortillas are crisp around the edges and the house queso is bubbling and lightly golden. *We recommend lining the roasting tray with greaseproof paper. STEP 4 Meanwhile, place the saucepan containing the remaining consomme mixture over a medium heat and bring to a gentle simmer. Once hot, remove from the heat and season to taste with salt and pepper. Cut the lime in half. Cut one half into wedges and set aside for serving. Pick the coriander leaves from the stems and finely chop them. Combine with the reserved diced onion and a squeeze of juice from the remaining lime half. Season to taste with salt and pepper to make a quick salsa. STEP 5 As the birria tacos finish cooking in the oven, quickly rinse and wipe the frying pan used to sauté the mushrooms and onion. Return it to a medium-high heat and add a drizzle of olive oil. Once the tacos are ready, remove them from the oven, fold them in half, and place them in the frying pan. Fry on both sides until crispy, about 2 minutes per side. STEP 6 To serve, divide the birria tacos between serving plates and top with the onion, coriander and lime salsa . Serve with the remaining lime wedges on the side for squeezing over the tacos. Enjoy! What’s in your box Consomme House Queso Corn Tortillas Oyster Mushrooms Lime Coriander Brown Onion Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Crispy Mushroom Shawarma With Garlic Tahini Sauce, Pickled Radish & Fresh Mint In Soft Flatbread
method:
15 Plants Crispy Mushroom Shawarma With Garlic Tahini Sauce, Pickled Radish & Fresh Mint In Soft Flatbread Our most popular dish! Meaty portobello mushrooms are tossed in a shawarma marinade of cumin, allspice, pomegranate molasses and preserved lemon, roasted until crispy, then smothered in garlic tahini sauce, pickled radishes and fresh mint. All wrapped up in freshly baked, pillowy soft flatbread. Prep time 1 Total time 15 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan and take out a large roasting tray. STEP 2 Slice the portobello mushrooms 0.5cm thick. STEP 3 Place the portobello mushrooms into your roasting tray, pour over the mushroom marinade , a generous drizzle of olive oil, some salt and pepper and give everything a good mix with your hands until the they are all thoroughly coated. Roast in the oven for 15 minutes or until tender. STEP 4 Once the portobello mushrooms are ready, remove from the oven and place the flatbread(s) directly onto a shelf in your oven to warm through, around 1 minute. Be careful not to overcook as if they’re too crispy they won’t roll up well! STEP 5 Lay out your warm flatbreads , spread a layer of tahini dressing over the bottom, pile on the mushroom shawarma , radish pickle and finish with some jalapeñ o mint s alsa (very spicy!). Wrap them up and get stuck in! title: Sushi Bowl With Coconut Sesame Crusted Tofu, Edamame, Avocado & Gochujang Peanut Sauce
method:
high protein Sushi Bowl With Coconut Sesame Crusted Tofu, Edamame, Avocado & Gochujang Peanut Sauce This sushi bowl is packed with protein from the tofu and edamame, flavour from the gochujang peanut sauce and plenty of fresh, light and crunchy textures. The tofu is coated with a coconut, sesame and panko crust which is the perfect topping for sushi rice next to the creamy avocado and crunchy carrot. Prep time 8 Total time 15 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Take out a frying pan and a saucepan that you have a lid for (if you have a steamer for it too, even better!). STEP 2 Rinse the sushi rice under cold water until the water runs mostly clear, then drain well. Add to a saucepan with 1.25~1.5x its volume of cold water and, if you have time, leave to soak for 10–15 minutes. Bring to a boil over medium heat, then cover, reduce the heat to low, and cook for 10 minutes without lifting the lid. Turn off the heat and leave to steam, covered, until ready to serve. STEP 3 Use a peeler to peel the carrots into ribbons. You could also grate the carrot on the biggest side of a box grater if you prefer. Dress it with a little olive oil, salt and pepper. STEP 4 Heat a drizzle of oil in your frying pan and add the sesame coconut mix . Stir often and fry on a medium low heat for a few mins until toasted and golden. Season to taste with salt and pepper, then place into a small bowl. Meanwhile, slice the tofu into 6 thin slices. Wipe the frying pan clean, add a drizzle of fresh oil and fry the tofu until just crispy on both sides, for about 2-3 mins. STEP 5 When the tofu is cooked, spread a thin layer of the gochujang peanut sauce onto one side of the tofu and press it into the sesame coconut mix to create a crust on one side. Repeat with the remaining tofu . Save any remaining crumb for topping. STEP 6 Cut the avocado in half and scoop it out of the skin with a spoon. Slice into thin slices. STEP 7 To serve, plate up the rice , edamame , carrot , avocado and crispy tofu into two bowls. Drizzle over the sauce and the remaining coconut sesame coconut mix . title: Ombar Mylk Chocolate
method:
Ombar Mylk Chocolate Ombar's Coco Mylk bar is incredibly creamy just like milk chocolate but without any of the dairy! The delightful blend of raw cacao coconut cream and caramel tasting coconut sugar is the secret to melt in the mouth goodness. And to top it off it's enriched with cacao polyphenols and billions of healthy live cultures! This product is fairly traded, gluten-free, organic and vegan. Cooking Mode:

title: One Pot Cheats Mushroom Lasagne With Beluga Lentils, Creamy Miso Bechamel & Green Kale
method:
gut health One Pot Cheats Mushroom Lasagne With Beluga Lentils, Creamy Miso Bechamel & Green Kale These are all the flavours and textures you love from a great lasagne, made in one pot. All you need to do is break the lasagne sheets into our rich mushroom and lentil ragu, add some dollops of our creamy miso bechamel and bake in the oven until melted and bubbling. Served with a side of curly kale so you're getting your greens in too. It's low in calories but packed with protein and fibre so will leave you feeling satisfied and full of energy. Prep time 2 Total time 25 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan and take out a large hob- and oven-proof casserole dish or deep ovenproof pan, along with a roasting tray. STEP 2 Dice the chestnut mushrooms into 0.5-1cm cubes. Remove any tough stems from the kale and tear the leaves into bite-sized pieces. STEP 3 Place your casserole dish or ovenproof pan over a medium-high heat and drizzle in some olive oil. Add the chestnut mushrooms and cook until golden brown. Drain the black lentils and add them to the pan along with the lasagne ragu sauce and 100ml water (200ml for 4 servings). Stir well and bring the mixture to a gentle boil. STEP 4 Break the lasagne sheets into 3–4 pieces each and stir them into the ragu, making sure they are separated and evenly distributed in the sauce. Push the pasta beneath the surface of the liquid, then cover with a lid or aluminium foil and bake in the oven for 10 minutes. Alternatively, you can prepare the ragu in a saucepan in Step 3, then transfer it to an ovenproof dish and layer with whole lasagne sheets instead of breaking them up and stirring them through the sauce. Bake in the oven for 20 minutes. STEP 5 Meanwhile, spread the prepared kale onto a roasting tray, drizzle with olive oil and season well with salt and pepper. Once the lasagne has baked for 10 minutes (or 20 minutes if using whole lasagne sheets), remove it from the oven and pour over the miso bechamel . Return to the oven, uncovered, for a further 10 minutes. At the same time, place the tray of kale onto the lower oven rack and roast for around 5 minutes until crisp at the edges, keeping an eye on it to ensure it doesn’t burn. Once the miso bechamel is golden on top and the kale is crisp, remove both from the oven. STEP 6 To serve, divide the lasagne and crispy kale between two plates. Enjoy! What’s in your box Lasagne Ragu Sauce Curly Kale Lasagne Sheets Black Lentils Miso Bechamel Chestnut Mushrooms Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Oyster Mushroom Bulgogi Bowl With Sticky Sushi Rice, Kimchi, Cucumber Salad & Toasted Sesame Seeds
method:
gut healthOyster Mushroom Bulgogi Bowl With Sticky Sushi Rice, Kimchi, Cucumber Salad & Toasted Sesame SeedsKorean food has some of the boldest, punchiest flavour profiles and bulgogi sauce is one of our favourites. This deliciously sticky, slightly spicy, umami-packed sauce is the perfect partner to our new favourite way to cook oyster mushrooms - skewered up and charred for that crispy BBQ flavour. Served over sticky sushi rice with refreshing cucumber salad and tangy gut-friendly kimchi. All finished with a sprinkle of sesame seeds and a final drizzle of that irresistible bulgogi sauce.Prep time4Total time15Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructionsBefore you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below).Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grainsbefore cooking or serving.STEP 1We highly recommend using a cast iron pan or griddle to char the oyster mushrooms if you have one. If you don't a frying pan will work too. As well as this, take out a saucepan and a mixing bowl. Boil your kettle for cooking the sushi rice.STEP 2Rinse thesushi riceunder cold water until the water runs mostly clear, then drain well. Add to a saucepan with 1.25~1.5x its volume of cold water and, if you have time, leave to soak for 10–15 minutes. Bring to a boil over medium heat, then cover, reduce the heat to low, and cook for 10 minutes without lifting the lid.Turn off the heat and leave to steam, covered, until ready to serve.STEP 3Dice thebaby cucumberinto bite-sized chunks and roughly chop thekimchi.STEP 4Tear theoyster mushroomsinto 1.5-2cm strips and add them to your mixing bowl along with 1/3rd of thebulgogi sauceand a good amount of salt and pepper. Give everything a good mix with your hands until the mushrooms are well coated in marinade. Thread the marinated oyster mushroom pieces onto thewooden skewersSTEP 5Place your cast iron griddle or frying pan over medium-high heat and add a generous drizzle of olive oil. Once the pan is searing hot, add the mushroom skewers. We recommend compressing the oyster mushrooms with a spatula or slotted turner so that they flatten and get crispier. Cook until crispy and blackened a little, around 5 minutes on each side.  Brush the mushrooms with a littlebulgogi sauceas you cook. We recommend saving half of the remaining bulgogi to use as a dressing when serving.STEP 6To serve, divide the cookedsushi ricebetween serving bowls and top withbulgogi oyster mushroom skewers,kimchi, andbaby cucumber. Finish with a sprinkle ofsesame garnishand a drizzle of the remaining bulgogi sauce. Enjoy!What’s in your boxBulgogi SauceOyster MushroomsKimchiBaby CucumberSushi RiceSesame GarnishWooden SkewersNutritional infoAll nutritional values are based on a per person scale.Calories454kcalTotal fat10.5gSaturated fat1.5gSalt3.5gTotal carbohydrate82.4gTotal sugar18.7gFibre6.9gProtein13.2gIngredients / AllergensKimchi (chinese leaf, daikon, carrot, spring onion, apple, chilli, tamari soy sauce, ginger, chilli powder, garlic, salt, live cultures), gluten-free low sodiumsoysauce, raw organic agave nectar, gochujang (water, brown rice, tapioca, red pepper powder, salt, alcohol,soybean, garlic, onion),sesameoil, rice vinegar, smoked paprika, oyster mushrooms, sushi rice, cucumber,sesameseedsFor allergens, please see ingredients inboldPlease be advised that we handle the following allergens in our kitchen: CELERY, SOYA, GLUTEN, NUTS, PEANUTS, MUSTARD, SESAME SEEDS, SULPHITES

title: Oyster Mushroom Shawarma Bowl With Bulgar Tabbouleh, Hummus, Sumac Pickled Cabbage, Cherry Tomatoes & Tahini Sauce
method:
gut health Oyster Mushroom Shawarma Bowl With Bulgar Tabbouleh, Hummus, Sumac Pickled Cabbage, Cherry Tomatoes & Tahini Sauce Meaty oyster mushrooms coated in our signature shawarma marinade featuring middle eastern spices and pomegranate molasses. At home you'll skewer them up and cook in a searing hot pan until charred and crispy. Served over bulgar and parsley tabbouleh with a healthy serving of creamy hummus, sumac pickled cabbage and onion, and juicy cherry tomatoes on the vine. All finished with fresh parsley and tahini sauce. Prep time 10 Total time 20 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 We highly recommend using a cast iron pan or griddle to char the oyster mushrooms if you have one. If you don't a frying pan will work too. As well as this, take out a saucepan and a mixing bowl. STEP 2 Place the bulgur wheat in a saucepan and cover with cold water. Bring to the boil, then reduce to a simmer and cook until tender but still al dente, about 6–8 minutes. Once ready, drain the bulgur wheat and lightly season with a drizzle of olive oil, a pinch of salt, and freshly ground black pepper. Set aside and keep warm. STEP 3 To make the hummus, add 3/4 of the tahini sauce and a whole tin of chickpeas along with half of the aquafaba (the liquid in the tin) to your blender and blend until smooth. Season to taste with salt and pepper. Set aside the rest of the tahini sauce for serving. We recommend using a blender for this process. However you could mash it with a masher or the back of a fork if you don't have one or prefer a chunkier consistency. STEP 4 Tear the oyster mushrooms into 1.5-2cm strips and add them to your mixing bowl along with the shawarma marinade and a good amount of salt and pepper. Give everything a good mix with your hands until the mushrooms are well coated in marinade. Thread the marinated oyster mushroom pieces onto the wooden skewers STEP 5 Place your cast iron griddle or frying pan over medium-high heat and add a generous drizzle of olive oil. Once the pan is searing hot, add the mushroom skewers. We recommend compressing the oyster mushrooms with a spatula or slotted turner so that they flatten and get crispier. Cook until crispy and blackened a little, around 5 minutes on each side. Brush the mushrooms with any remaining shawarma marinade as you cook. STEP 6 In the meantime, slice the cherry tomatoes into halves and finely chop the parsley leaves. Stir the chopped parsley through the bulgar wheat , along with a healthy drizzle of olive oil and season generously with salt and pepper. STEP 7 To serve, divide the bulgur tabbouleh between serving bowls and top it with an oyster mushroom skewer each, the cherry tomatoes , sumac pickled cabbage and the hummus on the side. Drizzle with the remaining tahini sauce all over. Enjoy! What’s in your box Shawarma Marinade Sumac Cabbage & Onion Garlic Tahini Sauce Oyster Mushrooms Cherry Tomatoes on the Vine Chickpeas Bulgar Wheat Parsley Wooden Skewers Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Oyster Mushroom Shawarma Tacos With Garlic Tahini Sauce, Chopped Shirazi Salad & Sumac Pickled Onions
method:
new dish Oyster Mushroom Shawarma Tacos With Garlic Tahini Sauce, Chopped Shirazi Salad & Sumac Pickled Onions Our famous mushroom shawarma reimagined in a Middle Eastern/Mexican fusion. Oyster mushrooms are roasted until crispy and meaty, then piled onto charred soft wheat tortillas with garlic tahini sauce and a chopped salad of tomato, cucumber and parsley. Finished with tangy sumac pickled pink onions. The flavours come together to make something so delicious, every taco leaves you craving another. Prep time 3 Total time 15 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 Preheat your oven to 180°C / 200°C fan, then take out a large roasting tray. STEP 2 Tear the oyster mushrooms into thin shreds with your hands and combine them with the shawarma marinade in a bowl. Spread them out thinly on your roasting tray in a single layer and roast in the oven for about 15 minutes, or until the edges become crispy and the marinade starts to caramelise. STEP 3 In the meantime, finely chop the baby cucumber , plum tomato , and parsley leaves, then combine them in a bowl with a sprinkle of salt and freshly ground pepper. Add a drizzle of olive oil if you like, and give everything a good toss. This is your chopped shirazi salad. STEP 4 Warm up the flour tortillas by dry-frying (without oil) in your frying pan over medium heat for about 30 seconds on each side. STEP 5 To serve, lay the flour tortillas on a large plate and spread a spoonful of garlic tahini sauce . Top with the chopped shirazi salad and drained sumac cabbage & onion , and finally the crispy shawarma oyster mushrooms . Enjoy! title: Pasta Alla Nerano With Regenerative Spelt Radiatori, Crispy Courgette, Peas, Tenderstem & Lemon Garlic Breadcrumbs
method:
Pasta Alla Nerano With Regenerative Spelt Radiatori, Crispy Courgette, Peas, Tenderstem & Lemon Garlic Breadcrumbs Our plant-based spin on the Campanian dish traditionally made with pasta, fried courgettes and cheese. We've added a super green herby sauce, plus extra greens like peas. We've also upgraded the pasta to spelt radiatori, made with grains from sustainable, British farms, using regenerative farming practices to improve biodiversity, soil health and nutrient density. All finished with zesty garlicky toasted breadcrumbs. Prep time 3 Total time 15 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 Boil the kettle and take out two saucepans and a wide frying pan. STEP 2 Place one of your saucepans over medium heat and pour in the boiling water from the kettle, adding a pinch of salt. Add the baby spinach to the boiling water and wilt for 1 minute, then transfer to the other saucepan using a slotted spoon. Bring the water back to the boil, add the spelt radiatori pasta, and cook for 11–12 minutes, or until al dente. Reserve a mugful of the cooking water, then drain and set aside. Cut the tenderstem broccoli into bite-sized pieces and add to the boiling water with the pasta for the final 3-4 minutes. STEP 3 Add the creamy green sauce to the saucepan with the wilted spinach and blend with a hand blender until smooth. Place over medium heat, add the garden peas , and cook until warmed through, about 5–6 minutes. Alternatively, if you don’t have a blender, finely mince the spinach and stir it into the sauce. STEP 4 Peel and finely mince the garlic clove, then add it to the frying pan with a drizzle of oil over medium heat. Once the garlic starts to turn golden, add the breadcrumb mix and reduce the heat to low. Zest the lemon and add it to the pan. Cook for a further 2–3 minutes, stirring occasionally, until golden and crispy. Remove from the pan and set aside. Cut the lemon in wedges for garnish. STEP 5 For the courgettes, wipe the frying pan clean, then place it over medium heat and add a generous drizzle of olive oil. We shallow fried in 0.5cm olive oil to make them very crispy, but you can sautee in less oil if you prefer. Slice the courgette into thin rounds (about 3 mm). Once the oil is hot, add the courgettes and fry until crispy at the edges. You may want to do this in batches to ensure the vegetables cook in a single layer for the best texture. STEP 6 Just before serving, add the cooked spelt radiatori pasta to the creamy green sauce over medium heat and combine well, using the reserved pasta cooking water to adjust the consistency. Divide between serving bowls and top with the courgette and tenderstem broccoli . Finish with a sprinkle of the garlic breadcrumbs and a squeeze of fresh lemon juice. Enjoy! title: Peanut Butter Ramen With Smoky Garlic & Sesame Tempeh, Pak Choi, Toasted Peanuts & Lime
method:
high protein Peanut Butter Ramen With Smoky Garlic & Sesame Tempeh, Pak Choi, Toasted Peanuts & Lime This one's for the ramen lovers. We've taken everything you know and love and taken it to a whole new level. Featuring a rich, moreish ramen broth that's simmered with coconut milk and peanut butter, and spiked with gochujang, a Korean fermented chilli paste that's full of flavour and great for your gut. At home you'll top it with smokey garlic and sesame tempeh mince, then nestle in noodles and greens for the ultimate healthy feast. Prep time 3 Total time 15 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Boil your kettle, then take out a bowl, a large frying pan and a large saucepan. STEP 2 Place your frying pan over medium heat and dry-toast the peanuts without oil until fragrant. Roughly chop them and set aside. STEP 3 Crumble the tempeh into small pieces in a bowl. Wipe clean the frying pan used for the peanuts, place it back over medium-high heat, and add a generous drizzle of cooking oil (we recommend roasted sesame oil). Add the crumbled tempeh and cook for about 6 minutes, until golden brown. Add the smoky tempeh marinade and stir well to coat all the pieces evenly. Continue cooking for a few more minutes, until fragrant and crispy at the edges. STEP 4 Meanwhile, fill your saucepans with boiling water from the kettle and place it over medium heat. Once the water is bubbling, add the flat rice noodles and cook for 5-6 minutes, or until al dente. Drain and rinse well under cold running water, then leave them in a colander to drain. Quickly rinse the saucepan for the next step. STEP 5 Slice the base off the pak choi , separate the leaves, and wash them thoroughly. If any leaves are very large, slice them in half lengthwise. Pour the peanut butter broth into a clean saucepan along with 200ml of water (double the amount for a four-person portion) and bring to a boil. Add the pak choi leaves, reduce the heat to a simmer, and cook until tender. Squeeze in the juice of half a lime at the end and stir well. For an extra kick of flavour, zest the lime and add it to the broth. STEP 6 To serve, divide the flat rice noodles into large bowls and ladle over the hot peanut butter broth with pak choi . Top with the tempeh , peanuts , and a squeeze of fresh lime . Enjoy! title: Peanut Butter & Raspberry Oat Pot 200g
method:
Peanut Butter & Raspberry Oat Pot 200gOur oat pots are the perfect on-the-go breakfast or healthy dessert. Creamy overnight oats nestled above moreish peanut butter, all topped with our sugar-free raspberry and chia seed jam that's the perfect balance between sweet and tart. title: Rich Porcini Lentil Ragu Over Wholewheat Rigatoni With Savoy Cabbage & Garden Peas
method:
high protein Rich Porcini Lentil Ragu Over Wholewheat Rigatoni With Savoy Cabbage & Garden Peas Delicious wholewheat rigatoni tossed through a rich and flavoursome ragu sauce slowly cooked with porcini mushrooms, garlic, red wine and thyme. Best of all, it's totally guilt free - packed with over 30g of protein and over 80% of your daily recommended fibre intake, thanks to the wholewheat pasta, lentils and a side of healthy greens and peas. Prep time 3 Total time 15 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Boil your kettle then take out two saucepans (ideally including one that has a steamer basket to go with it), and a deep frying pan or wok. STEP 2 Pour boiling water from your kettle into your larger saucepan and place over medium heat. Once it's bubbling add the wholewheat rigatoni and cook until al dente, around 12 minutes. Scoop out a mug full (approx 100ml) of pasta water, then drain the pasta. STEP 3 Place your deep frying pan or wok back over a medium heat. Meanwhile finely dice the chestnut mushrooms into approx 1cm cubes - you want to dice rather than slice to get a mince-like texture for the sauce. Add a drizzle of olive oil to your pan and fry off the mushrooms until they are browned off and tender, around 5-7 minutes. STEP 4 Fill a little boiling water in your other saucepan and place the steamer basket over the top. While it is brought back to a boil, cut the savoy cabbage into large chunks then steam until wilted, around 10 minutes. Add the garden peas for the final 3 minutes of cooking time. If you don't have a steamer you can boil instead for the same amount of time. Season the greens well with salt and pepper and stir through some vegan butter or extra virgin olive oil (optional). STEP 5 Once the diced mushrooms are fried off, drain the lentils then add them to the pan along with the porcini ragu sauc e and cook until piping hot, for around 3-4 minutes. Once the pasta is cooked, add into the pan along with some of the reserved pasta water to loosen up the sauce a little. Stir everything well to combine. We’ve kept this recipe healthy, but you could add some vegan butter and cheese to the pasta at this point if you wanted make it more indulgent! STEP 6 To serve up, divide the rigatoni in porcini ragu between serving bowls and serve the savoy cabbage and garden peas alongside. Enjoy! What’s in your box Porcini Ragu Sauce Chestnut Mushrooms Organic Brown Lentils Wholewheat Rigatoni Garden Peas Savoy Cabbage Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Pumpkin Laksa With Crispy Oyster Mushrooms, Pak Choi, Carrots & Udon Noodles
method:
Pumpkin Laksa With Crispy Oyster Mushrooms, Pak Choi, Carrots & Udon Noodles This creamy, Thai-inspired noodle soup contains everything you need for a nourishing, delicious dinner. The rich pumpkin broth is lightly spiced and stirred through with creamy coconut milk for richness and filled with a medley of healthy vegetables including crispy oyster mushrooms, pak choi, ribboned carrots and fresh spring onion. Prep time 4 Total time 15 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables. STEP 1 Boil your kettle, then take out a large frying pan and two saucepans. STEP 2 Peel the carrot into ribbons. Slice the pak choi into quarters lengthways, keeping the stem in tact if you can. STEP 3 In a separate saucepan, add the pumpkin broth along with 200~250ml of water (or 400~500ml for a 4 Person box) and heat through until piping hot. Zest the lime into the broth at the end and season to taste with salt. STEP 4 Place your saucepan over medium-high heat and fill it with the boiling water from the kettle. Once the water returns to a boil, blanch the carrot ribbons for one minute, then remove them with a slotted spoon and set aside. Next, blanch the pak choi for two minutes, then remove and set aside. Add the udon noodles to the boiling water and cook for 5–6 minutes. Drain the noodles well. STEP 5 Place your frying pan over medium heat and add a drizzle of oil (use sesame oil if you have some, otherwise any oil will do). Meanwhile, tear the oyster mushrooms into strips and then add them to the pan once it's hot. Fry for 5-7 minutes or until golden and starting to crisp up. Add the mushroom spice mix along with a dash of soy sauce if you have some, if not add a pinch of salt. Cook for another 1-2 minutes, then remove from the heat. Meanwhile, finely slice the spring onion and cut the lime in half. STEP 6 To assemble, divide the udon noodles between serving bowls, and pour the pumpkin broth over. Top with the carrot , mushrooms , pak choi and finish with a squeeze of fresh lime juice and sliced spring onions . Enjoy! What’s in your box Pumpkin Laksa Udon Noodles Oyster Mushrooms Mushroom Spice Mix Pak Choi Carrot Lime Spring Onions Nutritional info All nutritional values are based on a per person scale. title: Red Lentil Pasta Bake With Butter Beans, Tenderstem Broccoli & Cheesy Miso Bechamel
method:
Red Lentil Pasta Bake With Butter Beans, Tenderstem Broccoli & Cheesy Miso BechamelA delicious and comforting pasta bake the whole family will love, featuring gluten free red lentil pasta which is packed with protein and fibre. It bubbles away in a rich tomato and red pepper sauce with butter beans and tenderstem broccoli for extra fibre and protein. All finished with creamy miso bechamel which gives a final golden, cheesy top.Prep time5Total time20Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructionsBefore you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables.Please note: the finished meal image on this page shows red lentil maccheroncelli, but pasta shapes may vary (such as fusilli or pennette) depending on availability. Quality, taste, and nutrition stay exactly the same.Also, this week you’ll receive seasonalpurple sprouting broccoliinstead of tenderstem. The cooking method and time remain the same. Enjoy!STEP 1Preheat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan and boil your kettle. Take out a saucepan, a wide oven dish, and a small baking tray.STEP 2Place the saucepan over medium heat and fill it with the boiling water from the kettle. Season generously with salt. Once boiling, add thered lentil pastaand cook for 6 minutes or until al dente. Reserve a mugful of pasta water, then drain and set aside.Be careful not to overcook, as the pasta will continue cooking in the oven.STEP 3In an oven dish, combine thetomato & red pepper sauce,butter beans(including the brine from the tin), and the cookedred lentil pasta. Once everything is well mixed and spread evenly in the dish, pour themiso bechamelover the top. Place the dish in the oven and bake for 15–20 minutes, until the sauce is bubbling and the top is golden brown.STEP 4Cut thepurple sprouting broccoliinto bite-sized pieces and toss with a drizzle of oil, salt and pepper. Spread it out on a baking tray and place it on the bottom shelf of the oven, roasting for 10–15 minutes.Alternatively, you can add the broccoli to the pasta bake in STEP 3 and cook everything together, or boil it in lightly salted water for 5–6 minutes until al dente, using the same saucepan you used for the pasta.STEP 5To serve, divide thepasta bakebetween bowls and serve with a side of roastedbroccoli. Finish with a drizzle of olive oil and a sprinkle of salt and freshly ground black pepper. Enjoy!What’s in your boxTomato & Red Pepper SauceMiso BechamelButter BeansTenderstem BroccoliRed Lentil PastaNutritional infoAll nutritional values are based on a per person scale.Calories502kcalTotal fat7.5gSaturated fat1.4gSalt1.5gTotal carbohydrate83gTotal sugar13.5gFibre14.6gProtein25.1gIngredients / AllergensRed lentil pasta (red lentil flour, wholemeal rice, yellow maize, white maize, rice, maize fibre, potato starch), sage, oregano, garlic, tomato puree, basil, chopped tomatoes, red pepper, sea salt, raw organic agave nectar, black pepper, balsamic vinegar, extra virgin olive oil, rapeseed oil,soymilk, nutritional yeast, potato flakes, ground garlic, ground onion, rice vinegar, miso (soy), lemon, butter beans, tenderstem broccoliFor allergens please see ingredients inboldPlease be advised that we handle the following allergens in our kitchen: CELERY, SOYA, GLUTEN, NUTS, PEANUTS, MUSTARD, SESAME SEEDS, SULPHITES

title: Blistered Cherry Tomato Orecchiette, With Borlotti, Creamy 'Burrata', Crispy Garlic & Fresh Basil
method:
new dish Blistered Cherry Tomato Orecchiette, With Borlotti, Creamy 'Burrata', Crispy Garlic & Fresh Basil Cherry tomatoes slowly blistered with fresh garlic to create a naturally sweet, umami-rich tomato sauce. At home you'll stir through fresh basil and a combination of wholewheat orecchiette pasta and tender borlotti beans, which make this dish higher in protein and fibre, which is great for your gut. Finished with a healthy dollop of our homemade plant-based burrata, made with all-natural ingredients including live cultures and organic soy. Finished with crispy garlic oil and a final flourish of fresh basil leaves. Prep time 1 Total time 15 Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructions Before you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see 'what’s in your box' section below). Thoroughly wash all fresh vegetables, leaves, salad ingredients and raw grains before cooking or serving. STEP 1 Boil your kettle and take out two saucepans. Fill one of the saucepans with boiling water from the kettle, salt it generously, and place it over medium heat. STEP 2 Add the wholewheat orecchiette pasta to the pan of boiling salted water and cook until al dente, about 10 minutes. Reserve a mugful of the cooking water, then drain the pasta. STEP 3 Place the other saucepan over low heat and add a generous glug of good-quality olive oil. Peel and thinly slice the garlic cloves , then add them to the oil. Spread the slices out in an even layer and let them sizzle for 2–3 minutes, until golden and crispy. Once ready, transfer the garlic to a small bowl along with some of the oil. STEP 4 Return the saucepan to medium-high heat with the residual garlic oil. Add the cherry tomatoes and let them cook undisturbed for 2 minutes to blister on the bottom. Stir, then cook for another 3-4 minutes or until they've softened a little. Lower the heat to medium, then add the tomato sauce and the drained, rinsed borlotti beans . Cook for around 2-3 minutes, then add the basil leaves, reserving a few for garnishing. Fold in the cooked orecchiette and use the reserved pasta cooking water to adjust the consistency as needed. STEP 5 To serve, divide the blistered cherry tomato orecchiette with borlotti beans between bowls and top each with a dollop of cashew ‘burrata’ . Finish with the remaining basil leaves, a drizzle of the crispy garlic oil , and season with sea salt and black pepper to taste. Enjoy!
